## PAGE 00683: Document view modes

- page_id: `247047736`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047736/Document+view+modes
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-05T14:10:46.920+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Structure of the document portal
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can view a Cameo Collaborator document in several different view modes:

- Model view : In this view mode, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space=''] of a document portal shows only the content of the item selected in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Navigation pane' space=''] . In addition, t he Model view shows model information and is the default mode of a Cameo Collaborator document.
- Document view : This mode hides model details and allows you to browse a Cameo Collaborator document continuously. You can scroll through the entire content of the selected model view, or select an item in the navigation pane to load the related content.
- **Wiki view**: Similar to the Document view, The Wiki viewhides model details while displaying the content of just one section, like the Model view.

To change the view mode of a Cameo Collaborator document

1. In the app bar of a Cameo Collaborator document, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the model view whose view mode you want to change.
2. On the right side of the app bar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
3. In the open menu, click **Active view** and select the desired view mode. 
 
 [IMAGE alt='' src='']

After changing the document view mode, the selected model view is reloaded and displayed in a new mode.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

In addition, t<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">he </span>Model view<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> shows model information and is the default mode of a Cameo Collaborator document.</span></li><li><strong>Document view</strong>: This mode hides model details and allows you to browse a Cameo Collaborator document continuously. You <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">can scroll through the entire content of the selected model view, or select an item in the navigation pane to load the related content.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Wiki view</strong>: <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">Similar to the Document view, The Wiki view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>hides model details while displaying the content of just one section, like the Model view.</span></span></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To change the view mode of a Cameo Collaborator document</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">In the app bar of a Cameo Collaborator document, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="11"><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_view_list.png" /></ac:image>  and select the model view whose view mode you want to change.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">On the right side of the app bar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">In the open menu, click </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Active view</strong> and select the desired view mode.<br /><br /> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_document_view_mode.png" /></ac:image></span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><br />After changing the document view mode, the selected model view is reloaded and displayed in a new mode.</span></p>
````

## PAGE 00684: Downloadable scripts

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page lists the scripts needed to install and configure the products.

Before using any of the scripts below, make sure to review the linked documentation.

| File | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and components installation script package. |
|  | Scripts to back up and restore the Cassandra database. |
|  | The script package with utilities for gathering and managing log files on a Teamwork Cloud server. |
|  | Scripts to upgrade Apache Tomcat and JDK when hardening Web Application Platform. |
|  | Scripts to install Monitoring Stack. |
|  | The Admin Tools package which is a set of Linux utility scripts to facilitate server-side tasks. |
|  | Script to configure port access for the firewall when hardening Cassandra. |
|  | Sample scripts to deploy nGinx and HAProxy proxies for Teamwork Cloud. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047976 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00685: Downloading and uploading PDF/HTML templates

- page_id: `247047976`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047976/Downloading+and+uploading+PDF+HTML+templates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:49.726+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Sharing and exporting > Customizing PDF/HTML templates
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

PDF/HTML templates for exported documents are document-specific. You can download the default PDF/HTML template directly from a Cameo Collaborator document, make any desired changes, and then upload the modified template back to the document. In addition, a single document can hold multiple PDF/HTML templates, enabling you to generate different outputs from the same document.

To download the default PDF/HTML template

1. Open the document whose PDF/HTML template you want to download.
2. On the right side of the app bar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select About .
3. On the left side of the open screen, select PDF/HTML templates .
4. In the Default template card, click the Download button. [ATTACHMENT filename='downloading_default_pdf_html_template.png']

The default PDF/HTML template for the open document is downloaded to your machine and you can customize it to meet your specific needs.

To upload a custom PDF/HTML template

1. Open the document to which you want to upload a custom PDF/HTML template.
2. On the right side of the app bar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select About .
3. On the left side of the open screen, select PDF/HTML templates .
4. In the bottom right corner of the screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='add_icon.png'] .
5. In the Template title box of the open dialog, type the template name.
6. Drag the template file to the dialog or click the file icon to find the file on your machine. [ATTACHMENT filename='uploading_pdf_html_template.png']
7. When the template file is uploaded, click the Save button.

The uploaded template is displayed in the **PDF/HTML templates** screen.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=301368745 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00686: Downloading permissions report

- page_id: `301368745`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/301368745/Downloading+permissions+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-23T14:01:34.108+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Admin web applications > Users application
- labels: ['twc', 'mcs']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can download a user's permission report and view the permissions they were assigned in Excel format.

To download the permissions report, you must have the following permissions: **List All Resources**,******Manage Security Roles**, and**Manage User Permissions**.

To download the permissions report

Do one of the following:

- In the Users application, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to the user and select Generate permissions report. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- In the My account application, c lick [IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the bottom left corner to download the report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047830 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00687: Editing a comment

- page_id: `247047830`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047830/Editing+a+comment
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:19:02.243+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Working with comments > Working with comments in a modeling tool
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can edit comments in the Cameo Collaborator comments panel as described below.

To edit a comment

1. In a modeling tool, open the model related to the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document with comments you want to edit.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening the comments panel' space=''].
3. In the comments panel, find the comment you want to edit.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the right side of the comment to show available actions.
5. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom of the comment and select **Edit**. The **Edit comment** pane opens.
6. In the open pane, edit the comment title and/or comment text.
7. If needed, click the High priority switch on the bottom of the Edit comment pane to change the comment priority.
8. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] on the top right corner of the pane to save the changes.

##### Discarding comments

If you do not want to save comment changes after starting to edit the comment, you can cancel the editing process.

To discard a comment

- After starting to edit a comment, click [ATTACHMENT filename='cancel_comment.png'] on the top left corner of the Edit comment pane to cancel the changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can edit comments in the Cameo Collaborator comments panel as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To edit a comment</p><hr /><ol><li><p>In a modeling tool, open the model related to the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document with comments you want to edit.</p></li><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Opening the comments panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Cameo Collaborator comments panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>In the comments panel, find the comment you want to edit.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand.png" /></ac:image> on the right side of the comment to show available actions.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom of the comment and select <strong>Edit</strong>. The <strong>Edit comment</strong> pane opens.</p></li><li>In the open pane, edit the comment title and/or comment text.</li><li>If needed, click the <strong>High priority</strong> switch on the bottom of the <strong>Edit comment</strong> pane to change the comment priority.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> on the top right corner of the pane to save the changes.</li></ol><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Discarding comments</h3><p>If you do not want to save comment changes after starting to edit the comment, you can cancel the editing process.</p><p><br /></p><p>To discard a comment</p><hr /><ul><li>After starting to edit a comment, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cancel_comment.png" /></ac:image> on the top left corner of the <strong>Edit comment</strong> pane to cancel the changes.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047392 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00688: Editing an LDAP server configuration properties

- page_id: `247047392`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047392/Editing+an+LDAP+server+configuration+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:37.627+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application > Managing LDAP servers
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can change the configuration properties of an LDAP server connected to the Teamwork Cloud system. The system will update the configuration properties with the new values automatically.

To edit the configuration properties of an LDAP server do one of the following

- On the **LDAP management** page, select card with LDAP server which you want to edit****and click **Edit**button.
  - Change wanted configuration parameters.
  - Click Save to save changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can change the configuration properties of an LDAP server connected to the Teamwork Cloud system. The system will update the configuration properties with the new values automatically.</p><p><br /></p><p>To edit the configuration properties of an LDAP server do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li><p>On the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">LDAP management</strong> page, select card with LDAP server which you want to edit<strong> </strong>and click <strong>Edit </strong>button. </p><ul><li>Change wanted configuration parameters.</li><li>Click <strong>Save</strong> to save changes. </li></ul></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047867 space=MCS version=3 -->
## PAGE 00689: Editing and contextualizing

- page_id: `247047867`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047867/Editing+and+contextualizing
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-08T13:11:28.944+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can edit certain model elements right in a Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document. This is very beneficial for the whole product team, because engineers and even stakeholders can make changes to a model without having to use a modeling tool. In addition, the model editing capability speeds up the collaboration on a model. It allows you to instantly correct property values in a Cameo Collaborator document and skip the commenting process.

The sections below explain several main workflows where the model editing feature can be used.

#### NOTE: Documents published from synchronized projects

Documents published from synchronized projects

If a Cameo Collaborator document is published from a synchronized project on the target Teamwork Cloud server, the commenting and model editing capabilities in the document will be disabled so that the synchronized project on the target server cannot be modified. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cross-cluster resource synchronization' space=''].

##### Model editing to provide feedback

If you need to review a Cameo Collaborator document and provide feedback on the model, follow the workflow below.

To provide feedback on a model

1. Open the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document you want to review.
2. Review the document, including its structure, element characteristics, and diagrams.
3. Do one or several of the following actions:
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with comments in Cameo Collaborator' space=''] for items that need to be addressed.
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing element values' space=''] if you know what they should be.
4. Inform the author that you have finished reviewing the document so that he/she could react to your feedback.

##### Model editing to react to feedback

When a reviewer provides feedback on a Cameo Collaborator document, address the comments right in the document without having to use a modeling tool.

To react to feedback

1. Open the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document that has comments you need to address.
2. In the app bar click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Comments pane' space=''] and read the comments.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing element values' space=''] in the document to address the reported issues.
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Replying to comments' space=''] to inform the reviewer that required changes have been made or ask for additional information.
5. Inform the reviewer that you have finished updating the model and ask for another round of review.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

This is very beneficial for the whole product team, because engineers and even stakeholders can make changes to a model without having to use a modeling tool. In addition, the model editing capability speeds up the collaboration on a model. It allows you to instantly correct property values in a Cameo Collaborator document and skip the commenting process.</p><p>The sections below explain several main workflows where the model editing feature can be used.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40ec8c45-01b7-4413-a02f-f98fbae18aba"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Documents published from synchronized style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Review the document, including its structure, element characteristics, and diagrams.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Do one or several of the following actions:</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with comments in Cameo Collaborator" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create textual or graphical comments]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for items that need to be addressed.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing element values" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Edit element values]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> if you know what they should be.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Inform the author that you have finished reviewing the document so that he/she could react to your feedback.</span></li></ol><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Model editing to react to feedback</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">When a reviewer provides feedback on a Cameo Collaborator document, address the comments right in the document without having to use a modeling tool.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To react to feedback</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document that has comments you need to address.</li><li><p>In the app bar click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show-hide_comments_pane.png" /></ac:image> to open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Comments pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[comments pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and read the comments.</p></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing element values" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Edit element values]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in the document to address the reported issues.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Replying to comments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Reply to the comments]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to inform the reviewer that required changes have been made or ask for additional information.</li><li>Inform the reviewer that you have finished updating the model and ask for another round of review.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047221 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00690: Editing custom roles

- page_id: `247047221`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047221/Editing+custom+roles
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:07:38.837+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Admin web applications > Roles application > Managing roles
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating roles' space=''], you can modify it by changing its name, description, and permissions as described in the following sections.

#### NOTE: Editing roles

Editing roles

- You can edit only custom (user-created) roles.
- You must have the Security Manager role to edit custom roles.

##### Editing role details

In the Roles application, you can edit the name and description of a custom role as described below.

To edit role details

1. Do one of the following actions:
  - In the content pane of the Roles application, select a role and when the Role pane opens, click the Edit button in the role details card. [ATTACHMENT filename='editing_role_details.png']
  - In the content pane of the Roles application, click [ATTACHMENT filename='actions.png'] next to a role name and select Edit role details .
2. When the Edit role details pane opens, change role name and or description and click [ATTACHMENT filename='save_role.png'] .

##### Changing role permissions

You can use the Roles application to add or remove [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='']of a custom role. Note that a role must have at least one permission.

To add or remove permissions of a role

1. Do one of the following actions:
  - In the content pane of the Roles application, select a role and when the Role pane opens, click the Change button in the role details card.
  - In the content pane of the Roles application, click [ATTACHMENT filename='actions.png'] next to a role name and select Change permissions .
2. When the Change permissions pane opens, select the permissions you want to assign to the role (as shown below) and/or clear the check-boxes next to the permissions you want to remove. [ATTACHMENT filename='change_permissions_pane.png']
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save_role.png'] to save the changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>After <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating roles" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[creating a role]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, you can modify it by changing its name, description, and permissions as described in the following sections.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ec4ae845-3894-4d42-9db0-051dbab57fa6"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Editing roles</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can edit only custom (user-created) roles.</li><li>You must have the Security Manager role to edit custom roles.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Editing role details</h3><p>In the Roles application, you can edit the name and description of a custom role as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To edit role details</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following actions:<ul><li>In the content pane of the Roles application, select a role and when the <strong>Role</strong> pane opens, click the <strong>Edit</strong> button in the role details card.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="editing_role_details.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>In the content pane of the Roles application, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actions.png" /></ac:image> next to a role name and select <strong>Edit role details</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>When the <strong>Edit role details</strong> pane opens, change role name and or description and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_role.png" /></ac:image>.</li></ol><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Changing role permissions</h3><p>You can use the Roles application to add or remove <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Permissions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[permissions ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>of a custom role. Note that a role must have at least one permission.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add or remove permissions of a role</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following actions:<ul><li>In the content pane of the Roles application, select a role and when the <strong>Role</strong> pane opens, click the <strong>Change</strong> button in the role details card.</li><li>In the content pane of the Roles application, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actions.png" /></ac:image> next to a role name and select <strong>Change permissions</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>When the <strong>Change permissions</strong> pane opens, select the permissions you want to assign to the role (as shown below) and/or clear the check-boxes next to the permissions you want to remove.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="change_permissions_pane.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_role.png" /></ac:image> to save the changes.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047870 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00691: Editing element values

- page_id: `247047870`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047870/Editing+element+values
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:20:31.758+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Editing and contextualizing
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you need to provide feedback on a model or address reviewer's comments, edit certain element values right in the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document.

#### NOTE: Important

Important

- In order to edit element values in a Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing documents' space=''] .
- Before starting editing element values, read about [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model editing behavior and constraints' space=''] to understand how this feature works.

To edit element values in a Cameo Collaborator document

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space=''].
3. On the content pane, select the item you want to edit. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
4. In the **Edit** pane (displayed below), change the item value and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The example shows how you can edit requirement text.

Once you finish the steps described above, the modified item value is saved both in the Cameo Collaborator document and Teamwork Cloud project. See the figures below for more examples of editing different types of values.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Editing a Boolean type of value.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Editing an Enumeration type of value.

#### TIP: Deleting property values

Deleting property values

To delete the value of an editable property:

1. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space=''].
2. On the content pane, select the item you want to delete. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
3. When the **Edit** pane opens, click next to the value you want to delete.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the changes.

##### Reordering the values of multi-value properties

You can use the model editing feature to reorder the values of multi-value properties as described below.

To reorder the values of multi-value properties

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space=''].
3. On the content pane, select the item you want to edit. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
4. When the **Edit** pane opens, drag a value to the desired position as displayed below. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298095454 space=MCS version=7 -->
## PAGE 00692: Element chooser service

- page_id: `298095454`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/298095454/Element+chooser+service

- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide
- labels: ['twc', 'mcs']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Element chooser service allows you to select model elements in a modeling tool using the web UI. You need to run this service to be able to use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using server projects' space='CM']functionality.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using the Element chooser in modeling tool.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The Element chooser service allows you to select model elements in a modeling tool using the web UI. You need to run this service to be able to use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CM" ri:content-title="Using server projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Partial project usage ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>functionality.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="element_chooser.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Using the Element chooser in modeling tool.</span></h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047019 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00693: Enabling and disabling users

- page_id: `247047019`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047019/Enabling+and+disabling+users
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:30.201+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Admin web applications > Users application > Managing users > Managing users details
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is possible to disable login for user. To enable or disable user login you must have **User manager** role.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Easily distinguish enabled user from disabled.

To enable/disable user login, do one of the following

- Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the user and from menu select to Disable user/Enable user .
- Go to User pane and select to edit user details,
  - Change sliders position next to Enabled (slide left to disable, slide right to enable user`s login).
- Go to User pane and click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on Account card, Actions menu will open.
  - From menu select to Disable user/Enable user.

If you disable a user's access while he or she is online or logged in, Teamwork Cloud Admin will not log them out immediately. Changes to access permission will take effect immediately once the user logs out.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is possible to disable login for user. To enable or disable user login you must have <strong>User manager</strong> role. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="enabled_disabled_users.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Enabling and disabling users" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Easily distinguish enabled user from disabled.</h6><p>To enable/disable user login, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"> <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Enabling and disabling users" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </span> next to the user and from menu select to <strong>Disable user/Enable user</strong>.</li><li>Go to <strong>User</strong> pane and select to edit user details,<ul><li> Change sliders position next to <strong>Enabled </strong>(slide left to disable, slide right to enable user`s login). </li></ul></li><li>Go to <strong>User</strong> pane and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Enabling and disabling users" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on <strong>Account </strong>card, <strong>Actions</strong> menu will open. <br /><ul><li>From menu select to <strong>Disable user/Enable user. </strong></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6f37d2c2-3674-43dd-9dd0-560cee5f9f94"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If you disable a user's access while he or she is online or logged in, Teamwork Cloud Admin will not log them out immediately. Changes to access permission will take effect immediately once the user logs out.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047289 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00694: Enabling conditional user groups

- page_id: `247047289`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047289/Enabling+conditional+user+groups
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:35.852+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application > Managing server settings
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Using conditional user groups allows you to validate/invalidate user group membership based on SAML attribute values. When users are added to a conditional user group, they receive the group permissions only if the group condition is met, for example, if they log in from the right location. For instructions on how to create conditional user groups, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Conditional user groups' space='MCS'].

To allow users to create conditional user groups, first, you have to enable this feature in the Settings application as described below.

To enable conditional user groups

1. Go to the Settings application and select **Server settings** on the left side of the screen.
2. In the User management card, turn on the **Conditional user groups** option.

Once conditional user groups are enabled, you can set a user group as conditional and determine user access rights dynamically by SAML IDPs on each login operation by taking network and user location (and possibly other criteria) into account.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Enabling conditional user groups.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Using conditional user groups allows you to validate/invalidate user group membership based on SAML attribute values. When users are added to a conditional user group, they receive the group permissions only if the group condition is met, for example, if they log in from the right location. For instructions on how to create conditional user groups, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Conditional user groups" /></ac:link>.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To allow users to create conditional user groups, first, you have to enable this feature in the Settings application as described below.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To enable conditional user groups</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Go to the Settings application and select <strong>Server settings</strong> on the left side of the screen.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the User management card, turn on the <strong>Conditional user groups</strong> option.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Once conditional user groups are enabled, you can set a user group as conditional and determine <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">user access rights dynamically by SAML IDPs on each login operation by taking network and user location (and possibly other criteria) into account.</span></span></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="enabling_conditional_user_groups.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Enabling conditional user groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Enabling conditional user groups.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047286 space=MCS version=4 -->
## PAGE 00695: Enabling external user creation

- page_id: `247047286`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047286/Enabling+external+user+creation
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-03-13T12:32:15.424+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application > Managing server settings
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **User management**section of **Server settings**has the options to manage the external user creation process.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Managing external user creation options.

- If you enable the Create external user on successful authentication option, an external user will be created upon successful authentication. This means that external users will be able to log in to Teamwork Cloud Admin even if no user existed before. An external user will be created upon such login. If you disable this option, no external users will be created if the user tries to log in.

After an external user is created, it takes some time for it to show up inthe **User** application list. This delay is caused by the properties caching.

- Once you enable external user creation, you can also enable the Default user permissions template . With this option enabled, external users created upon successful authentication are added to the Default Template user group and given its permissions and assignments.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>User management </strong>section of <strong>Server settings </strong>has the options to manage the external user creation process.</p><p style="text-align: center;"> <ac:image ac:height="220"><ri:attachment ri:filename="user_management.jpg" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Managing external user creation options.</h6><ul><li>If you enable the<strong> Create external user on successful authentication </strong>option, an external user will be created upon successful authentication. This means that external users will be able to log in to Teamwork Cloud Admin even if no user existed before. An external user will be created upon such login.<br />If you disable this option, no external users will be created if the user tries to log in.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78a82261-7405-43f9-9dfb-b128f1cf1369"><ac:rich-text-body><p>After an external user is created, <span>it takes some time for it to show up in </span>the <strong>User</strong> application list. This delay is caused by the <span>properties</span> caching.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>Once you enable external user creation, you can also enable the <strong>Default user permissions template</strong>. With this option enabled, external users created upon successful authentication are added to the Default Template user group and given its permissions and assignments.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047270 space=MCS version=4 -->
## PAGE 00696: Enabling secure connection between client and server

- page_id: `247047270`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047270/Enabling+secure+connection+between+client+and+server
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-12-22T09:12:16.593+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application > Managing server settings
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

By default, communication between the Teamwork Cloud server and client is unencrypted. This page provides information on how to encrypt communication using TLS over TCP.

##### Enabling TLS encryption on server side

To enable a secure connection to Teamwork Cloud, you must enable TLS (Transport Layer Security) on the Teamwork Cloud Admin's **Server Settings** page. This page also allows you to disable the option if you do not need to use a secure connection (see the following figure). You can always enable it whenever necessary. The most straightforward way to set up encryption is to use the keystore generated for Teamwork Cloud and/or Web Application Platform. Locate and download the server Java keystore (default: *<install_root>/Certs**/keystore.p12*). Make a note of the password used for the keystore. If there is a corresponding public certificate (e.g. *teamworkcloud.crt*) on the server, download it as well. If there is no public certificate, use the following command to extract the public certificate from the Java keystore:

```bash
-keystore  -file ]]>
```

If you need to generate a new keystore, see [Regenerating self-signed keystore](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCS/Managing+SSL+certificate#ManagingSSLcertificate-Regeneratingself-signedkeystore).

The unencrypted port (default: 3579) remains active even after encryption is enabled. To prevent unencrypted connections, block the port at the firewall level.

1. Go to the Settings app.
2. Move the slider to the right to enable the TLS protocol.
3. Enter a custom port for encrypted communication, or keep default value of 10002.
4. Upload the Java keystore file, and type the password.
5. Click Save .

##### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Enabling TLS protocol in Teamwork Cloud.

##### Setting up client-side TLS

Server Certificates

The default port for a secure connection is 10002. If you are using another port for a secure connection instead of the default, append the port number to the server name in the **Login** dialog:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The unecrypted port (3579) remains active even after TLS is enabled. Teamwork Cloud will continue to accept communication through both encrypted and unencrypted ports. Use firewall to disable port 3579 and block unencrypted communication.

#### EXPAND: Generating a new key pair manually

Generating a new key pair manually

To enable a secure connection between the client and the server, you need to generate two keystore files, each in their own keystores:

- keystore.jks (contains key and certificate) – server-side – upload it using Teamwork Cloud Admin console.
- cert.jks (contains public certificate) – client-side – add it using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Server Certificates' space='MDTWRT'] option group in Environment Options.

1. Go to your Java directory and open the folder named bin .
2. Enter keytool -genkey -alias MyDomain -keyalg RSA -keystore keystore.jks -keysize 2048 and press Enter . You can give any name to*MyDomain* and **keystore.jks**.
3. Enter a password for your keystore and answer the questions asked. A certificate with a key is generated and placed into the keystore.
4. To extract a certificate without a key, type keytool -export -alias MyDomain -file certificate.cer -keystore keystore.jks and press Enter .
5. To place a certificate into another keystore, e.g. cert.jks , type keytool -import -alias MyDomain -file certificate.cer -keystore cert.jks and press Enter . You can give any name to **cert.jks**. However, by default, the tool searches for this exact name. Two keystore files are generated: one for the server ( keystore.jks ) and one for the client ( cert.jks ). You can now proceed to enable a secure connection between the client (modeling tool) and the server (Teamwork Cloud).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>By default, communication between the Teamwork Cloud server and client is unencrypted. This page provides information on how to encrypt communication using TLS over TCP.</p><h3>Enabling TLS encryption on server side</h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="883be2ec-c8f0-48e4-a645-7e01e9463656">To enable a secure connection</ac:inline-comment-marker> to Teamwork Cloud, you must enable TLS (Transport Layer Security) on the Teamwork Cloud Admin's <strong>Server Settings</strong> page. This page also allows you to disable the option if you do not need to use a secure connection (see the following figure). You can always enable it whenever necessary. The most straightforward way to set up encryption is to use the keystore generated for Teamwork Cloud and/or Web Application Platform. <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Locate and download the server Java keystore (default: <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/Certs</em></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><em>/keystore.p12</em>). Make a note of the password used for the keystore. If there is a corresponding public certificate (e.g. <em>teamworkcloud.crt</em>) on the server, download it as well. If there is no public certificate, use the following command to extract the public certificate from the Java <span>keystore</span>:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ec4149b3-edfc-4638-9615-c21e2bb76fa4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[keytool -export -alias <teamworkcloud> -keystore <keystore.p12> -file <teamworkcloud.crt>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81d2bf01-d7ee-414c-b837-b008ca6ae249"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you need to generate a new keystore, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCS/Managing+SSL+certificate#ManagingSSLcertificate-Regeneratingself-signedkeystore">Regenerating self-signed keystore</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="16cd75e5-08bb-4b6c-895d-c0558e82a18d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The unencrypted port (default: 3579) remains active even after encryption is enabled. To prevent unencrypted connections, block the port at the firewall level.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span>To enable a secure connection using the TLS protocol in Teamwork Cloud Admin<hr /><ol><li>Go to the <strong>Settings</strong> app.</li><li>Move the slider to the right to enable the TLS protocol. </li><li>Enter a custom port for encrypted communication, or keep default value of 10002.</li><li>Upload the <strong>Java <span>keystore </span></strong>file, and type the password.</li><li>Click <strong>Save</strong>.</li></ol><h3 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="WAP_Secure_Connection.jpg" /></ac:image></h3><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">Enabling TLS protocol in Teamwork Cloud</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">.</span></h6><h3>Setting up client-side TLS</h3>To enable a secure connection using the TLS protocol on the client-side, use the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MDTWRT/Server+Certificates">Server Certificates</a> tool and provide the public certificate.<ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc7b6091-11cb-4b03-926c-04d2152afeeb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The default port for a secure connection is 10002. A certificate with a key is generated and placed into the keystore. </li><li>To extract a certificate without a key, type <em>keytool -export -alias MyDomain -file certificate.cer -keystore <span>keystore</span>.jks</em> and press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li><li>To place a certificate into another keystore, e.g. <strong>cert.jks</strong>, type <em>keytool -import -alias MyDomain -file certificate.cer -keystore cert.jks</em> and press <strong>Enter</strong>. <ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4db5da94-0333-4f2f-b369-8d4ec95b4c0d"><ac:rich-text-body>You can give any name to <strong>cert.jks</strong>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047672 space=MCS version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00697: Enabling the Document View

- page_id: `247047672`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047672/Enabling+the+Document+View
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:42.889+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Publishing documents > Document templates > Working with custom document templates
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

TheDocument Viewis the mode of a published project that allows you to view it as a continuous document. Essentially, by using theDocument View,you can scroll through the entire content of a project view instead of jumping from one navigation tree element to another as in the Model View.TheDocument Viewcan be enabled for individual views of a project. After selecting a view,you are presented with the document containing information from that view. The sections of the document are numbered hierarchically both in the Navigation pane and the content pane.

The Document View can be enabled by customizing the template that will be used for publishing or from the title bar of a published project.

To enable the Document View in the template used for publishing

1. Open the View Diagram of the template that you intend to use for publishing.
2. Double-click the element with the «view» stereotype. In the View Diagram of the template, each element with the «view» stereotype represents a separate navigation tree of a published project. If your template has more than one such element, you can enable the Document View for each of them separately.
3. In the open Specification window, set the **Document View** property as *true.* [ATTACHMENT filename='view_properties_specification_window.png'] The figure shows the Specification window of the element with the «view» stereotype with the Document View enabled.
4. Click the **Close** button.
5. Save the template file.

The Document View is enabled for the selected view of a published project.

#### NOTE: Important

Important

The **Document View** property can also be enabled in the Specification window of the element with the «Document» stereotype. Doing this overrides the property values set for individual elements with the «view» stereotype.

To enable the Document View from the title bar of a published project

1. In the app bar of a published model, click [ATTACHMENT filename='model_view_list.png'] and select the model view for which you want to enable the Document View mode.
2. On the right side of the app bar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Document View .

The Document View is enabled for the selected navigation tree of a published project.

When the mode is set from the title bar, it's only valid until the refresh of a web portal.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The sections of the document are numbered hierarchically both in the Navigation pane and the content pane.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The Document View can be enabled by customizing the template that will be used for publishing or from the title bar of a published project.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To enable the Document View in the template used for publishing</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the View Diagram of the template that you intend to use for publishing.</li><li><p>Double-click the element with the «view» stereotype.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="26f4b52b-1f5e-45e4-b3a0-c01e43945ffb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the View Diagram of the template, each element with the «view» stereotype represents a separate navigation tree of a published project. Doing this overrides the property values set for individual elements with the «view» stereotype.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To enable the Document View from the title bar of a published project</p><hr /><ol><li>In the app bar of a published model, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_view_list.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Enabling the Document View" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the model view for which you want to enable the Document View mode.</li><li>On the right side of the app bar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Enabling the Document View" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Document View</strong>.</li></ol><p><br />The Document View is enabled for the selected navigation tree of a published project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9403120-b3ac-4031-8451-d2d1e4e2b109"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When the mode is set from the title bar, it's only valid until the refresh of a web portal.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047680 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00698: Enabling the Single Tree View

- page_id: `247047680`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047680/Enabling+the+Single+Tree+View
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:43.077+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Publishing documents > Document templates > Working with custom document templates
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Single Tree View is the mode of a published model that allows you to render different model views as the nodes of a single navigation tree. This mode enables you to navigate a document without having to switch between different views.

To enable the Single Tree View

1. Open the View Diagram of the template that you intend to use for publishing.
2. Double-click the element with the «Document» stereotype.
3. In the open Specification window, set the Single Tree View property as true .
4. Click the Close button.
5. Save the template file.

When you publish a model using this template, all the views of a published model are rendered in a single navigation tree. To access the content of the desired view, simply expand the node of that view in the Navigation pane.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Single Tree View is the mode of a published model that allows you to render different model views as the nodes of a single navigation tree. This mode enables you to navigate a document without having to switch between different views. </p><p><br /></p><p>To enable the Single Tree View</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the View Diagram of the template that you intend to use for publishing.</li><li>Double-click the element with the «Document» stereotype.</li><li>In the open Specification window, set the <strong>Single Tree View</strong> property as <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Close</strong> button.</li><li>Save the template file.</li></ol><p><br />When you publish a model using this template, all the views of a published model are rendered in a single navigation tree. To access the content of the desired view, simply expand the node of that view in the Navigation pane.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046637 space=MCS version=6 -->
## PAGE 00699: Encrypting property values

- page_id: `247046637`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046637/Encrypting+property+values
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-12-03T14:58:16.057+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Configuration > Hardening the deployment and its services
- labels: ['encrypt-properties', 'property-encryption', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Generating keys for encryption and decryption

By default, properties are stored as plain text. If required, any of these properties can be encrypted. For property encryption and decryption, you need to generate a pair of keys by using our property encryption tool. The tool is provided as the *encryptor.jar* file and the steps below explain how to use it.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

The encryption tool uses Java 21. Therefore, the system **PATH** variable needs to point to the location of the *bin*folder of the Java 21 home directory, for example, *C:\Java\Java11\bin* or */etc/alternatives/jre_21*.

#### NOTE: Note on special characters

Note on special characters

Encrypted properties (like passwords) can include special characters, such as exclamation marks, ampersands, or quotes. These characters must be escaped because the shell that runs the command interprets them within the property and does not invoke the encryptor.The characters that need to be escaped and the escape method depend on the command line shell used to run the command, socheck your shell documentation on how to escape the property value if it contains special characters.

The following examples use the property value **!&**:

- Invoking the command in the Linux bash shell: texttrue

- Invoking the command in the Windows Command Shell: texttrue

To encrypt properties

1. Click the link to download the [ATTACHMENT filename='encryptor-2026x.jar'] [ATTACHMENT filename='encryptor.jar'] file.
2. Open the command-line interface and navigate to the directory where the encryptor.jar file is located.
3. Run the java -jar encryptor.jar command. You should see the information on how to use the tool as displayed below. [ATTACHMENT filename='encryptor_open.png']
4. Run the **java -jar encryptor.jar generate-keys <path_to_folder_for_keys>** command to generate a pair of keys for property encryption and decryption. Make sure to replace the**<path_to_folder_for_keys>**placeholder with the actual path to the directory where you want to store the keys.You should see a confirmation that the keys have been successfully generated. Keys for encryption and decryptionAfter executing the command, the following keys are created in the specified directory:**propertiesEncryptionKey.pub** - a public key used to encrypt property values.**propertiesEncryptionKey** - a private key used to decrypt property values.
5. To encrypt a property, run the java -jar encryptor.jar encrypt <unencrypted_property_value> <path_to_the public_key> command. Make sure to replace the <unencrypted_property_value> and <path_to_the public_key> placeholders with the actual property value and the path to the propertiesEncryptionKey.pub file. The tool will output the encrypted property value. [ATTACHMENT filename='encryptor.png']

##### Configuring Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloudencrypted property values

To configure Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloudencrypted property values

1. Go to the <install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/configuration/ directory and edit the application.conf file.
2. In the *application.conf*file, replace the actual property value with the encrypted property value in the following format: **ENC(encrypted_property_value)**. Example of an encrypted propertyFor example, an encrypted Cassandra password, should look similar to this one:**password=ENC("lcvPbmmI32vn1jD2EYrQfMLu7ydX+/DW8wljMsk/ 
+UcjrPWXELau1YC1FFTa3UBMptu3sFK6wjOuLipveVBJYzo0k+yfgt1qnD1ud/3E7LsRGwMe4srlAIF7Kfq36pPywTu58NBYp6M8yktIDTtKtQSvuZF8Qcb38Vw56OkGCd9Io+vdM5aTN 
TNi2ls7jnN09BuqD6lZvwJ/atcrd0grfEhxwM3PSw0zzUS+EenjWedTxogxpfXLtUbTEuzwYSMGqyi5goX9Wb3vnkMPs7xlL/ZhqYYIQCXwQaREbzLIH3oJEHJxRvUpFIhWJoc/7LJfn/09ukx0wjgF296kEgI+rA==")**.
3. In the same *application.conf* file, add the **esi.config.decrypt_key_file******property and specify the path to the private key (the*propertiesEncryptionKey* file) as its value, e.g., **esi.config.decrypt_key_file=*<install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/**TeamworkCloud/keys/**propertiesEncryptionKey***. Private key locationThe private key (the *propertiesEncryptionKey* file) should be in a location that Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloudcan access.
4. Restart Teamwork Cloud service.

##### Configuring Web Application Platform encrypted property values

To configure Web Application Platform encrypted values

1. Go to the <install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory and open the webappplatform.properties file.
2. In the *webappplatform.properties* file, replace the actual property value with the encrypted property value in the following format: **ENC(encrypted_property_value)**. Example of an encrypted propertyFor example, an encrypted Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloudadministrator password should look similar to this one:**twc.admin.password=ENC(lcvPbmmI32vn1jD2EYrQfMLu7ydX+/DW8wljMsk/ 
+UcjrPWXELau1YC1FFTa3UBMptu3sFK6wjOuLipveVBJYzo0k+yfgt1qnD1ud/3E7LsRGwMe4srlAIF7Kfq36pPywTu58NBYp6M8yktIDTtKtQSvuZF8Qcb38Vw56OkGCd9Io+vdM5aTN 
TNi2ls7jnN09BuqD6lZvwJ/atcrd0grfEhxwM3PSw0zzUS+EenjWedTxogxpfXLtUbTEuzwYSMGqyi5goX9Wb3vnkMPs7xlL/ZhqYYIQCXwQaREbzLIH3oJEHJxRvUpFIhWJoc/7LJfn/09ukx0wjgF296kEgI+rA==)**.
3. Go to the <install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/conf directory and open the catalina.properties file.
4. In the *catalina.properties* file, add the **properties.encryption.private.key.location** property and specify the path to the private key (the*propertiesEncryptionKey* file) as its value, e.g., **properties.encryption.private.key.location=*<install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/WebApp**Platform/conf/keys/propertiesEncryptionKey****.* Private key locationThe private key (the *propertiesEncryptionKey* file) should be in the location which Web Application Platform can access.
5. Restart Web Application Platform service.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Generating keys for encryption and decryption</h3><p>By default, properties are stored as plain text. If required, any of these properties can be encrypted. For property encryption and decryption, you need to generate a pair of keys by using our property encryption tool. The tool is provided as the <em>encryptor.jar</em> file and the steps below explain how to use it.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3117ade9-4860-4557-acb9-5fc322b36a4b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The encryption tool uses Java 21. Therefore, the system <strong>PATH</strong> variable needs to point to the location of the <em>bin</em><span> folder</span> of the Java 21 home directory, for example, <em>C:\Java\Java11\bin</em> or <em>/etc/alternatives/jre_21</em>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="372ac1bb-9c88-40b4-a084-4aac35ea6e3c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note on special characters</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Encrypted properties (like passwords) can include special characters, such as exclamation marks, ampersands, or quotes. These characters must be escaped because the shell that runs th</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">e command interprets them within the property and does not invoke the encryptor.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The characters that need to be escaped and the escape method depend on the command line shell used to run the command, so </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">check your shell documentation on how to escape the property value if it contains special characters.</span></p><p style="text-align: left;">The following examples use the property value <strong>!&amp;</strong>:</p><ul class="alternate" style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="43ea6f0b-1cf7-4005-91f1-c1f14df5f93b">Invoking the command in the Linux bash shell:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d4517de-776f-4b58-bf41-19396423734d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[java -jar encryptor.jar encrypt '!&']]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><ul class="alternate" style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="8bbad6cd-467a-4fc5-98fa-80396c936fd5">Invoking the command in the Windows Command Shell:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1836d100-6e47-47e3-a552-4d56d5e37d25"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[java -jar encryptor.jar encrypt "!&"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro> </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To encrypt properties</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the link to download the<ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="encryptor-2026x.jar" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><em><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="encryptor.jar" /></ac:link></em> file.</li><li>Open the command-line interface and navigate to the directory where the <em>encryptor.jar</em> file is located.</li><li>Run the <strong>java -jar encryptor.jar</strong> command. You should see the information on how to use the tool as displayed below.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="900"><ri:attachment ri:filename="encryptor_open.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Run the <strong>java -jar encryptor.jar generate-keys &lt;path_to_folder_for_keys&gt;</strong> command to generate a pair of keys for property encryption and decryption. M<span>ake sure to replace the </span><strong>&lt;path_to_folder_for_keys&gt;</strong><span> placeholder with the actual path to the directory where you want to store the keys. </span>You should see a confirmation that the keys have been successfully generated.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="876d7480-836d-49fb-8183-eefd51c7a612"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Keys for encryption and decryption</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>After executing the command, the following keys are created in the specified directory:</p><ul><li><strong>propertiesEncryptionKey.pub</strong> - a public key used to encrypt property values.</li><li><strong>propertiesEncryptionKey</strong> - a private key used to decrypt property values.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>To encrypt a property, run the <strong>java -jar encryptor.jar encrypt &lt;unencrypted_property_value&gt; &lt;path_to_the public_key&gt;</strong> command. Make sure to replace the <strong>&lt;unencrypted_property_value&gt;</strong> and<strong> &lt;path_to_the public_key&gt;</strong> placeholders with the actual property value and the path to the <em>propertiesEncryptionKey.pub</em> file. The tool will output the encrypted property value.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="900"><ri:attachment ri:filename="encryptor.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ol><h3>Configuring <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud </span>encrypted property values</h3><p>To configure <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud </span>encrypted property values</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">&lt;install_root&gt;</span>/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/configuration/</em> directory and edit the <em style="text-align: left;">application.conf </em>file.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <em>application.conf </em>file, replace the actual property value with the encrypted property value in the following format: <strong>ENC(encrypted_property_value)</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2e3c19e4-8921-4e37-880e-345bc54d9237"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example of an encrypted property</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><span>For example, an encrypted Cassandra password, should look similar to this one: </span><strong>password=ENC(&quot;lcvPbmmI32vn1jD2EYrQfMLu7ydX+/DW8wljMsk/<br />+UcjrPWXELau1YC1FFTa3UBMptu3sFK6wjOuLipveVBJYzo0k+yfgt1qnD1ud/3E7LsRGwMe4srlAIF7Kfq36pPywTu58NBYp6M8yktIDTtKtQSvuZF8Qcb38Vw56OkGCd9Io+vdM5aTN<br />TNi2ls7jnN09BuqD6lZvwJ/atcrd0grfEhxwM3PSw0zzUS+EenjWedTxogxpfXLtUbTEuzwYSMGqyi5goX9Wb3vnkMPs7xlL/ZhqYYIQCXwQaREbzLIH3oJEHJxRvUpFIhWJoc/7LJfn/09ukx0wjgF296kEgI+rA==&quot;)</strong>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the same <em>application.conf</em> file, add the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>esi.config.decrypt_key_file</strong><strong> </strong></span>property and specify the path to the private key (the<em> propertiesEncryptionKey</em> file) as its value, e.g., <strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">esi.config.decrypt_key_file=<em style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">&lt;install_root&gt;</span>/CATIANoMagicServices/</em><em>TeamworkCloud/keys/</em></span><em>propertiesEncryptionKey</em></strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd243f64-8a04-4d9b-b879-093d108fd151"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Private key location</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The private key (the <em>propertiesEncryptionKey</em> file) should be in a location that <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud </span>can access.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Restart Teamwork Cloud service.</li></ol><h3>Configuring Web Application Platform encrypted property values</h3><p>To configure Web Application Platform encrypted values</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">&lt;install_root&gt;</span>/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf </em>directory and open the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file, replace the actual property value with the encrypted property value in the following format: <strong>ENC(encrypted_property_value)</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c1c77c78-c806-4722-8882-ad45b415ab81"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example of an encrypted property</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><span>For example, an encrypted <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud </span>administrator password should look similar to this one: </span><strong>twc.admin.password=ENC(lcvPbmmI32vn1jD2EYrQfMLu7ydX+/DW8wljMsk/<br />+UcjrPWXELau1YC1FFTa3UBMptu3sFK6wjOuLipveVBJYzo0k+yfgt1qnD1ud/3E7LsRGwMe4srlAIF7Kfq36pPywTu58NBYp6M8yktIDTtKtQSvuZF8Qcb38Vw56OkGCd9Io+vdM5aTN<br to the <em style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">&lt;install_root&gt;</span>/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/conf</em> directory and open the <em>catalina.properties</em> file.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <em>catalina.properties</em> file, add the <strong>properties.encryption.private.key.location</strong> property and specify the path to the private key (the<em> propertiesEncryptionKey</em> file) as its value, e.g., <strong>properties.encryption.private.key.location=<em style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">&lt;install_root&gt;</span>/CATIANoMagicServices/WebApp</em><em>Platform/conf/keys/propertiesEncryptionKey</em></strong><em>.</em></p><ac:structured-macro ## PAGE 00700: Enterprise deployment
````

- page_id: `247046564`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046564/Enterprise+deployment
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-03-02T15:21:41.328+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Web Application Platform as well as all of its plugins are provided as separate web applications. Therefore, it is possible to containerize applications and this way achieve better performance and scalability.

To learn how to containerize Web Application Platform applications using Docker, see the following topics:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The enterprise deployment option for Teamwork Cloud and its services.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Web Application Platform as well as all of its plugins are provided as separate web applications. Therefore, it is possible to containerize applications and this way achieve better performance and scalability.</p><p>To learn how to containerize Web Application Platform applications using Docker, see the following topics:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="1af390c3-7a92-4d14-bb6a-49fc02d6d986" /></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="WAP &amp; TWC enterprise deployment.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The enterprise deployment option for Teamwork Cloud and its services.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047970 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00701: Exporting comments to PDF and HTML

- page_id: `247047970`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047970/Exporting+comments+to+PDF+and+HTML
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:49.472+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Sharing and exporting
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud allows you to export comments to the PDF/HTML format. This way you can archive the discussions among team members that resulted in specific changes made in the model and thus trace the reasoning behind these changes. The exported files include not only all comments and replies created in the document but also the document and project information and comments statistics as shown below.

To export Cameo Collaborator comments to PDF or HTML

1. Open the Cameo Collaborator document containing the comments you want to export.
2. On the right side of the app bar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Export document to PDF or HTML .
3. In the open wizard, click the Type drop-down box and select Comments .
4. Click the Document scope drop-down box and select one of the following scope options:
  - Active section - exports the comments in the document's open section, which is selected in the Navigation pane.
  - Active section's presentation elements - selecting this option allows you to choose from which elements of the active section comments are exported. [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_comment_scope.png']
  - Active view - exports the comments in the open view of the document which is selected in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='App bar' space='MCS'] .
  - Whole document - exports all the comments in the document.
5. Click the Next button.
6. In the second step of the wizard, specify the following export properties:
  - Choose the format of the target document by selecting the **PDF** or **HTML** radio button.
  - To add a cover page, turn on the**Cover page**switch. A cover page contains the document name, export date, project information, and document information.
  - To add a page with the table of contents, turn on the**Table of contents**switch . Adding the table of contents works only when exporting comments to the PDF format.
  - To protect the generated file with a password, turn on the **Password protected** switch and enter the desired password in the **Password** box. Enabling password protection works only when exporting comments to the PDF format.
  - If you want to use a custom template to generate a PDF or HTML, click the **Template** drop-down box and select the template you want to use. If the document has no custom templates, the drop-down box is disabled and the default template is used. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Click the Finish button.
8. When you get a message that the file is ready to download, click the Download button.

Note that exporting a large number of comments may take a while, so be patient while the file is being generated. You can check the file status in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing long-running tasks' space='MCS'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

