<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047575 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00721: Identifying suspicious usages

- page_id: `247047575`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047575/Identifying+suspicious+usages
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:12:14.555+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Resource Usage Map
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the Resource Usage Map application, you can detect three types of suspicious usages: inconsistent version usages, cyclic usages, and unused resources. In addition, you can filter and investigate suspicious usages by using the Filters pane. The sections below explain how to do that.

##### Inconsistent version usages

The inconsistent version usages filter allows you to see which resources use different versions of the same resource directly and through other used projects. You can investigate every inconsistent version usage displayed in your resource usage map as described below.

To investigate an inconsistent version usage

1. In the Filters pane of a resource usage map, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the inconsistent version usages filter to see the list of all the inconsistent version usages in the map.
2. Select the inconsistent version usage you want to investigate. A detailed map of that usage is created in the content pane.
3. Click the **Details** button on the top right corner of the inconsistent version usage map, to see full information of the selected usage as illustrated in the following figure. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As you can see in the example map below, *Car Structure library* directly uses version 20.0 of *MD_customization_for_SysML* but other resources used by *Car Structure library* use version 19.0 v9 of *MD_customization_for_SysML* (indirect usage).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of an inconsistent version usage map.

##### Cyclic usages

The cyclic usages filter allows you to see which resourcesuse themselves through other used projects and form a usage loop. You can investigate every cyclic usage displayed in your resource usage map as described below.

To investigate a cyclic usage

1. In the Filters pane of a resource usage map, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the cyclic usages filter to see the list of all the cyclic usages in the map.
2. Select the cyclic usage you want to investigate. A detailed map of that usage is created in the content pane.
3. Click the **Details** button on the top right corner of the cyclic usage map, to see full information of the selected usage as illustrated in the following figure. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As you can see in the example map below, *MD_customization_for_SysML* uses itself by using *ISO-80000* which uses *QUDV_SysML 1.4* and *QUDV_SysML 1.4* uses *MD_customization_for_SysML* thus forming a usage loop.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of a cyclic usage map.

##### Unused resources

The unused resources filter allows you to see which resources are not used by other resources stored in the Teamwork Cloud repository. Thisfilter can only be displayed in a map created for all the resources in the repository.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In the Resource Usage Map application, you can detect three types of suspicious usages: inconsistent version usages, cyclic usages, and unused resources. In addition, you can filter and investigate suspicious usages by using the Filters pane. The sections below explain how to do that.</p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Inconsistent version usages</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The inconsistent version usages filter allows you to see which resources use different versions of the same resource directly and through other used projects. You can investigate every inconsistent version usage displayed in your resource usage map as described below.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To investigate an inconsistent version usage</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Filters pane of a resource usage map, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand.png" /></ac:image> next to the inconsistent version usages filter to see the list of all the inconsistent version usages in the map.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the inconsistent version usage you want to investigate. A detailed map of that usage is created in the content pane.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>Details</strong> button on the top right corner of the inconsistent version usage map, to see full information of the selected usage as illustrated in the following figure.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="inconsistent_version_usage_details.png" /></ac:image></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br />As you can see in the example map below, <em>Car Structure library</em> directly uses version 20.0 of <em>MD_customization_for_SysML</em> but other resources used by <em>Car Structure library</em> use version 19.0 v9 of <em>MD_customization_for_SysML</em> (indirect usage).</p><p><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="inconsisten_version_usages.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="line-height: 1.66667;letter-spacing: normal;text-align: center;">An example of an inconsistent version usage map.</h6><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Cyclic usages</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The cyclic usages filter allows you to see which resources </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">use themselves through other used projects and form a usage loop</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. You can investigate every cyclic usage displayed in your resource usage map as described below.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To investigate a cyclic usage</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Filters pane of a resource usage map, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand.png" /></ac:image> next to the cyclic usages filter to see the list of all the cyclic usages in the map.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the cyclic usage you want to investigate. A detailed map of that usage is created in the content pane.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>Details</strong> button on the top right corner of the cyclic usage map, to see full information of the selected usage as illustrated in the following figure.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cyclic_usage_details.png" /></ac:image></span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br />As you can see in the example map below, <em>MD_customization_for_SysML</em> uses itself by using <em>ISO-80000</em> which uses <em>QUDV_SysML 1.4</em> and <em>QUDV_SysML 1.4</em> uses <em>MD_customization_for_SysML</em> thus forming a usage loop.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="cyclic_usages.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="line-height: 1.66667;letter-spacing: normal;text-align: center;">An example of a cyclic usage map.</h6><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Unused resources</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The unused resources filter allows you to see which resources are not used by other resources stored in the Teamwork Cloud repository. This</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> filter can only be displayed in a map created for all the resources in the repository.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="unused_resources.png" /></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046942 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00722: Importing users and groups

- page_id: `247046942`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046942/Importing+users+and+groups
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:28.773+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Admin web applications > Users application
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can import external users and user groups from LDAP servers to Teamwork Cloud.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

- To import users or user groups at least one LDAP directory must be added to Teamwork Cloud (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding and configuring LDAP server' space='MCS']).
- Because of the LDAP export limit, you can only import 1000 users at once.
- It is not possible to import users or user groups with the names that already exist as internal.

To import LDAP users or user groups

1. In the Users application, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
2. Select the LDAP server from which you want to import users and user groups and click OK .
3. In the search bar of the LDAP server pane, enter the name of the user or user group you want to import. If you want to get the list of all users stored in the selected LDAP server, type the asterisk sign (*) in the search bar.
4. Select the check-boxes next to the users and user groups you want to import.
5. Optionally click the Set roles button and assign specific roles to the users and user groups to be imported. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding roles' space='MCS'] .
6. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='import.jpg'] to start the import.

After completing the above steps, you will get a notification that the import process has started. If you import a large number of users at once, it may take a while before the import is finished.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Importing users and user groups in the Users application.

When imported users are new, external users are created and the selected roles are assigned to them (see step 5). If you import users that have been imported before, the existing user information is synchronized during the import process but the role assignments stay the same. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Synchronizing users and user groups' space='MCS'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can import external users and user groups from LDAP servers to Teamwork Cloud.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="98ca8cfd-64c6-41a3-ab0f-ef419be28c73"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To import users or user groups at least one LDAP directory must be added to Teamwork Cloud (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Adding and configuring LDAP server" /></ac:link>).<br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Because of the </span>LDAP export limit, you <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">can only import 1000 users at once.</span></li><li>It is not possible to import users or user groups with the names that already exist as internal.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To import LDAP users or user groups</p><hr /><ol><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Users application, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_users.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Importing users and groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="import_users_and_groups.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Importing users and groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</span></p></li><li>Select the LDAP server from which you want to import users and user groups and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li><p>In the search bar of the LDAP server pane, enter the name of the user or user group you want to import.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="11e0cbf0-1d36-4c78-a0eb-da879dc20c47"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If you want to get the list of all users stored in the selected LDAP server, type the asterisk sign (*) in the search bar.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Select the check-boxes next to the users and user groups you want to import.</li><li>Optionally click the <strong>Set roles</strong> button and assign specific roles to the users and user groups to be imported. For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Adding roles" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="import.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Importing users and groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to start the import.</li></ol><p><br />After completing the above steps, you will get a notification that the import process has started. If you import a large number of users at once, it may take a while before the import is finished.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:height="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="importing_users_and_groups.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Importing users and groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Importing users and user groups in the Users application.</h6><p>When imported users are new, external users are created and the selected roles are assigned to them (see step 5). If you import users that have been imported before, the existing user information is synchronized during the import process but the role assignments stay the same. For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Synchronizing users and user groups" /></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047413 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00723: Importing users and groups from LDAP

- page_id: `247047413`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047413/Importing+users+and+groups+from+LDAP
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:09:23.332+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application > Managing LDAP servers
- labels: ['importing-ldap-users', 'importing-ldap-group', 'synchronizing-users', 'synchronizing-groups', 'copy-assignment-in-ldap-form', 'copy-assignment-while-importing', 'assign-role-while-importing', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can import users and groups after all LDAP server configuration is completed. Teamwork Cloud importing also works as a user synchronization mechanism. Existing users will only be synchronized and new users in the Teamwork Cloud Admin will be imported. You can see user data in the **Users application**before choosing to import users. This importing and synchronization mechanism applies to groups as well. Data, including full name, phone, email, and department, will be synchronized.

##### Importing and synchronizing users and groups

To import/synchronize users and groups

1. On the selected LDAP server card click the **Import** button. The **Import LDAP user/group** pane opens.
2. Query all users/groups or specific users/groups and select them.
3. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='import_ldap_users.jpg'] button. New users/groups will be imported or existing users/groups will be synchronized. The existing user information will be updated but the role assignment stays the same.

##### Managing roles

You can assign roles in the same pane, while selecting users and groups to be imported.

###### Assigning roles

To assign a role from one user/group to another

1. On the Import LDAP user/group pane, next to Role assignments , click Set Roles .
2. In the Set roles pane, type a role name in the search bar.
3. Select a role. If it is a custom role, specify scope.
4. Click the Save button to save the changes.

###### Copying assignments

To copy assignment(s) from one user/group to another

1. On the Import LDAP user/group pane, next to Role assignments , click Set Roles .
2. In the Set roles pane, type a username whose assignments you want to copy in the search bar.
3. Select a user/group. The assignments associated with the selected user/group will appear.
4. Click the Save button to save the changes. The selected assignments will be assigned to the target user/group.

##### Troubleshooting

If you cannot synchronize users and/or groups, check the **server.log** file for a message warning about illegal characters. For example:

```text

```

If you see a similar message, allow the use of the character in the **application.conf** file:

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can import users and groups after all LDAP server configuration is completed. Teamwork Cloud importing also works as a user synchronization mechanism. Existing users will only be synchronized and new users in the Teamwork Cloud Admin will be imported. You can see user data in the <strong>Users application </strong>before choosing to import users. This importing and synchronization mechanism applies to groups as well. Data, including full name, phone, email, and department, will be synchronized. </p><h3>Importing and synchronizing users and groups</h3><p>To import/synchronize users and groups</p><hr /><ol><li><p>On the selected LDAP server card click the <strong>Import</strong> button. The <strong>Import LDAP user/group</strong> pane opens.</p></li><li>Query all users/groups or specific users/groups and select them. </li><li>Click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="27"><ri:attachment ri:filename="import_ldap_users.jpg" /></ac:image> button. New users/groups will be imported or existing users/groups will be synchronized. The existing user information will be updated but the role assignment stays the same.</li></ol><h3>Managing roles</h3><p>You can assign roles in the same pane, while selecting users and groups to be imported.</p><h4>Assigning roles</h4><p>To assign a role <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">from one user/group to another</span></p><hr /><ol><li>On the <strong>Import LDAP user/group</strong> pane, next to <strong>Role assignments</strong>, click <strong>Set Roles</strong>. </li><li>In the <strong>Set roles</strong> pane, type a role name in the search bar.</li><li>Select a role. If it is a custom role, specify scope.</li><li>Click the <strong>Save</strong> button to save the changes. </li></ol><h4>Copying assignments</h4><p>To <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">copy assignment(s) from one user/group to another</span></p><hr /><ol><li>On the <strong>Import LDAP user/group</strong> pane, next to <strong>Role assignments</strong>, click <strong>Set Roles</strong>. </li><li>In the <strong>Set roles</strong> pane, type a username whose assignments you want to copy in the search bar.</li><li>Select a user/group. The assignments associated with the selected user/group will appear.</li><li>Click the <strong>Save</strong> button to save the changes. The selected assignments will be assigned to the target user/group.</li></ol><h3>Troubleshooting</h3><p>If you cannot synchronize users and/or groups, check the <strong>server.log</strong> file for a message warning about illegal characters. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8202c58b-505a-47d4-9c76-eb2114437c58"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[INFO 2023-10-17 01:00:01.111Z CN=Name, OU=Users Has illegal character [\\]. Cannot be imported]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>If you see a similar message, allow the use of the character in the <span><strong>application.conf</strong> file:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b268c43-749e-47fc-bcec-5359bd1b4603"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[esi.ldap.allowBackSlashChar = true]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047929 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00724: Inserting content from another document

- page_id: `247047929`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047929/Inserting+content+from+another+document
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:48.400+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Editing and contextualizing > Contextualizing Cameo Collaborator documents
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can include content, such as diagrams and tables, from one document into another. Keep the included content up-to-date by selecting to display its latest version or include thecurrentcontent version to make sure it will stay the same.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

To add media to a Cameo Collaborator document:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing documents' space='MCS'] .
- You need the Read Resources permission or Resource Reviewer role for the document.
- You need the Read Resources and Edit Resources permission or Resource Contributor role for the project with read-write permissions for the project branch.

To insert content from another Cameo Collaborator document

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening a resource' space='MCS'].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space='MCS'].
3. Click the **Insert from document** icon in the location of an open document section where you want to include content. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the open wizard, select the document from which you want to insert the content.
5. In the next step, navigate to and select the document section from which you want to insert the content.
6. Select the element of the section you want to insert. You can only insert tables and diagrams.
7. Select one of the following insert settings:
  - Display latest version - automatically updates the inserted content to the latest version when it becomes available.
  - Display current version (at the time when inserting) - always displays the content version that was available at the time of insertion. [ATTACHMENT filename='insert_settings.png']
8. Click the Add button.
9. Repeat steps 3 to 8 to insert more content or click [ATTACHMENT filename='cancel_editing.png'] on the bottom right corner of the content pane to exit the editing mode.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


The following sections provide instructions on how to install and set up Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046456 space=MCS version=21 -->
## PAGE 00726: Installation on Linux

- page_id: `247046456`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046456/Installation+on+Linux
- version_number: 21
- version_date: `2026-03-04T15:21:03.350+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Installation
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following sections describe the full deployment of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud on a single Linux server. The deployment includes installation of third-party components: Apache Cassandra database and FlexNet license server. A set of installation scripts are provided to streamline the installation process. This type of all-in-one deployment should be used for testing and evaluation. For a performant system, we recommend distributing components and services on multiple servers.

The following Linux packages are required for each service. All these packages are automatically pulled from online repositories when using the included installation scripts. For offline installation, refer to the specific installation sections for more information on accessing these packages. Also refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='System requirements' space=''] section for version information.

For a complete list of service files, please see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Web Application Platform services' space=''].

| Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud |
| --- |
| Open JDK Java (see )Linux font package: dejavu-serif-fonts (optional) |
| Apache Cassandra |
| Open JDK Java (see )Cassandra and Cassandra Tools |
| FlexNet 11.19 |
| Existing Java installation (see )Linux utility package: unzipFlexNet 64-bit Installer and UtilitiesCameo Daemon for FlexNet |

**Prior to installation:**

- Make sure that your hardware and software meet the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='System requirements' space=''].
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Preparing the operating system' space=''] the Linux system for Cassandra database deployment.
- [Download](https://docs.nomagic.com/MCS/latest/magic-collaboration-studio/downloadable-scripts-283246728.html)and extract the installation script package *TWC_MCS_install_scripts_<version>.zip*
  - Extract the package in the same location as the installer .bin file
  - Add execute permission to all script files

The provided installation scripts are designed to streamline the installation process and automate system configuration. These scripts require remote access to official online repositories by default. Offline installation is possible if prerequisites are available locally on the system. For more information, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Product FAQ' space=''].

To install and configure a working instance of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud, follow the guides below:

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing Apache Cassandra' space=''] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing the FlexNet server' space=''] .
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing services' space=''] .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Product installation and configuration on a single Linux server.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046466 space=MCS version=7 -->
## PAGE 00727: Installation on Windows

- page_id: `247046466`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046466/Installation+on+Windows
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2025-12-03T14:16:54.542+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Installation
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Teamwork Cloud installation and configuration on a single Windows server.

This section provides instructions on how to install Cassandra and Magic Collaboration Studio /Teamwork Cloud on Windows. During the installation, the installer asks you to provide the required configuration values and creates a Windows service for you.

After completing the installation, make sure you grant access through the firewall to the required ports, as shown in the diagram above.

Once the installation is complete, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying the license' space=''] to start working with Teamwork Cloud in MagicDraw and using Teamwork Cloud Admin.

To install and set up Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloudon Windows, complete the following steps:

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='FlexNet license server installation and licensing' space='IL'] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing Cassandra on Windows' space=''] .
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing services on Windows' space=''] .
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting services on Windows' space=''] .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target" style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="teamwork_cloud_single_node_deployment.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Teamwork Cloud installation and configuration on a single Windows server.</h6><p>This section provides instructions on how to install Cassandra and <span>Magic Collaboration Studio / </span>Teamwork Cloud on Windows. During the installation, the installer asks you to provide the required configuration values and creates a Windows service for you.</p><p>After completing the installation, make sure you grant access through the firewall to the required ports, as shown in the diagram above.</p><p>Once the installation is complete, you need to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying the license" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[apply the Teamwork Cloud license]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to start working with Teamwork Cloud in MagicDraw and using Teamwork Cloud Admin.</p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To install and set up <span>Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud </span>on Windows, complete the following steps:</span></p><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="FlexNet license server installation and licensing" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Install FlexNet license server]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Installing Cassandra on Windows" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Install Cassandra on Windows]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Installing services on Windows" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Install the product on Windows]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Starting services on Windows" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Start Teamwork Cloud on Windows]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046461 space=MCS version=43 -->
## PAGE 00728: Installing Apache Cassandra

- page_id: `247046461`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046461/Installing+Apache+Cassandra
- version_number: 43
- version_date: `2026-05-27T08:39:29.038+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Installation > Installation on Linux
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Clouduses Apache Cassandra, an open-source NoSQL distributed database. Before installing Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud, please follow the steps below to install Apache Cassandra.

##### Prerequisites

- OpenJDK Java (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Third-Party Component Compatibility' space=''] for version information)
- Apache Cassandra RPMs
  - cassandra- <version_number> .noarch.rpm
  - cassandra-tools- <version_number> .noarch.rpm

##### Installing with script

The script downloads and installs the necessary packages, Cassandra, and the Cassandra tools from the Apache Software Foundation repository, and creates the necessary firewall rules to allow proper operation both for a single node or a cluster installation. The script will also install the required Java version and set it as the default system Java.

The script can also be used for offline installation, download the prerequisite RPM packages and place them in the same location as the installation script. Manually install required Java version and set it as the system default Java.

To install Apache Cassandra

1. Install Apache Cassandra by executing the*install_cassandra<version_number>_ol_rhel.sh* installation script. Exampletrue_ol_rhel.sh]]>
2. Start Apache Cassandra by executing the following command: bashDJangotrue
3. Check if Apache Cassandra is running by executing the following command: bashDJangotrue If Apache Cassandra is running, you should receive the output displayed below. If the service is fully operational, the first 2 characters of the last line are "UN", indicating that the node status isUp, and its state isNormal. bash Wait for a few minutes until Cassandra starts for the first time before checking if it is running. If Cassandra has not started yet, you will get the error: "No nodes present in the cluster. Has this node finished starting up?" This means that you need to give Cassandra more time to start.
4. If Apache Cassandra is not running or if you used installation options other than the one described in this chapter, optionally .

##### Developing a backup strategy

Before deploying Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloudand Apache Cassandra in a production environment, it is imperative to have a fully implemented backup strategy. The Cassandra database stores all project and user data associated with Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud. Review the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Backup and restore data procedures' space=''] document. Ensure that you test the entire backup and restore process before your deployment goes live to users.

During the backup process, user access to Cassandra should be suspended. Refer to the [Cassandra backup documentation](https://cassandra.apache.org/doc/4.0/cassandra/operating/backups.html) for more information.

Improper backup procedure can lead to total data loss! For example, taking an image snapshot of the storage system while Cassandra is actively accepting read and write requests will result in unrecoverable data.

##### Configuring Apache Cassandra for Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud

If you used other installation options and not the provided script or if Apache Cassandra does not start, configure it as described below.

install_cassandra<version_number>_<os_version>.sh

To configure Apache Cassandra

1. Locate the *cassandra.yaml* file (default: /etc/cassandra/conf/cassandra.yaml) and open for editing.
2. Find the following parameters related to the Cassandra node IP address and communication settings, and change them as shown below: bashExample
  - seeds - a comma-delimited list containing all of the seeds in the Cassandra cluster. Since our cluster consists of a single node, it contains only one entry - our IP address.
  - listen_address - the IP address that Cassandra uses to listen for connections.
  - broadcast_rpc_address - the IP address used to broadcast to other Cassandra nodes in the cluster. This parameter may be commented. In such a case, remove the "#" and ensure there are no leading spaces.
  - rpc_address - when set to 0.0.0.0, Cassandra listens to rpc requests on all interfaces.
3. Find the following parameters that control thresholds to ensure that the data being sent is processed properly, and change them as shown below: bashExample
4. To ensure that the default commit log size is 8GB (recommended), uncomment the**commitlog_total_space** parameter as shown below. bashExample Ensure that the partition where the commit log is installed has enough space to accommodate a commit log of 8GB.
5. To point the data to the appropriate locations, find the following parameters and change them as shown below: bashExample
6. Start Apache Cassandra by executing the following command: bashDJangotrue
7. Check if Apache Cassandra is running by executing the following command: bashDJangotrue If Apache Cassandra is running, you should receive the output displayed below. If the service is fully operational, the first 2 characters of the last line are "UN", indicating that the node status isUp, and its state isNormal. bashExample

##### Configuring Cassandra memory usage

If you did not use the installation script or want to increase the RAM usage by Cassandra, make the following changes. Otherwise, these configuration changes are set automatically by the Cassandra installation script.

Set the maximum direct memory size to ensure your off-heap structures (like memtables and caches) have a dedicated, stable pool of memory and prevent the node from crashing due to memory limits.

To configure the maximum direct memory size, you must explicitly set the standard JVM argument -XX:MaxDirectMemorySize, in the appropriate JVM options file jvmXX-server.options.

- Default file location: /etc/cassandra/conf/jvmXX-server.options
- You may need to add the -XX:MaxDirectMemorySize line if it is missing, or edit the existing one.
- Replace jvmXX with your installed Java version (read requirements for the deployed version).

**Edit jvmXX-server.options and add the line:**

```text

```

To configure the Java Heap space for Cassandra, please see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Configuring Java heap space' space=''].

##### Configuring Cassandra CPU usage

- In the jvmXX-server.options file, uncomment the following lines and set the values to the physical CPU core count (the values of both parameters should be the same):

```text

```

##### Additional configuration

- Synchronize CPU clocks on all Cassandra cluster nodes. Otherwise, you may encounter issues when creating an empty Cassandra cluster.
- When using cqlsh , the Python version required for cqlsh to work with Cassandra 5.0 is Python 3.8 to 3.11.
- In the logback.xml file, comment the "<appender-ref ref="ASYNCDEBUGLOG" />" line. This will increase Cassandra's performance by disabling the debug log.

##### Configuring Linux environment for Cassandra performance

If you install Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloudusing the *install_twc_mcs_centos_rhel.sh* script, Cassandra performance is tuned automatically. However, if you plan to use other installation options or if you need to set other parameters after running the script, you can do it manually as described in this section.

To improve Apache Cassandra performance

1. Locate the *sysctl.conf* file (default: /etc/sysctl.conf) and open it for editing.
2. To configure the TCP settings, add the following tuning parameters to the file: bashExample
3. To apply the setting without rebooting, execute the following command: bashDJangotrue

For more information about tuning Linux, see [DSE 6.9 Administrator Guide](https://docs.datastax.com/en/dse/6.9/managing/configure/recommended-settings.html).

###### Using jemalloc memory allocator

The jemalloc memory allocator package can potentially improve Cassandra performance. Our installation script does not install jemalloc. The easiest way to install this optional package is to first install the epel-release package. You can then pull the latest jemalloc release from the EPEL repository. An older version jemalloc is available for direct download and installation.

- EPEL package (optional, for pulling jemalloc package)

- jemalloc package (optional for performance)

It should start without any additional configuration.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To configure Apache Cassandra</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="145e460d-f0ca-4809-9036-628c1937aa5a"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Locate the <em>cassandra.yaml</em> file (default: /etc/cassandra/conf/cassandra.yaml) and open for editing.</p></li><li data-uuid="a4c23854-f940-45b9-9332-ccaa195b0535"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Find the following parameters related to the Cassandra node IP address and communication settings, and change them as shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b6a9752-4694-4d9c-ab86-1b951b51e430"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[seeds: "192.168.130.10"

````html
broadcast_rpc_address: 192.168.130.10

This parameter may be commented. In such a case, remove the &quot;#&quot; and ensure there are no leading spaces.</span></li><li><strong>rpc_address</strong> - when set <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">to </span>0.0.0.0, Cassandra listens <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">to rpc requests on all interfaces.</span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="14debb96-69b6-4098-bcc1-e6287cd10830"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Find the following parameters that <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">control thresholds to ensure that the data being sent is processed properly, and change them as shown below:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e4cb4630-dcd0-428b-a2e8-b52de1f74a28"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[commitlog_segment_size: 192MiB
read_request_timeout: 1800000ms
range_request_timeout: 1800000ms
write_request_timeout: 1800000ms
cas_contention_timeout: 1000ms
truncate_request_timeout: 1800000ms
request_timeout: 1800000ms
batch_size_warn_threshold: 3000KiB
batch_size_fail_threshold: 5000KiB]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li data-uuid="d11205c2-e6d9-4726-8f6f-8a95598e92c8"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To ensure that the default commit log size is 8GB (recommended), uncomment the </span><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="78cf8988-7eb0-4f64-bdff-00d0ebcf54ab">commitlog_total_space</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong> parameter as <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">shown below.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d71783fa-d7d3-4d31-9f6c-2b987cbe5147"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[commitlog_total_space: 8192MiB]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cff29604-57d5-48a5-8e32-b1435084a97c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Ensure that the partition where the commit log is installed has enough space to accommodate a commit log of 8GB.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li data-uuid="7e3df937-bcb9-41f9-9e57-d77fe04915bc"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To point the data to the appropriate locations, find the following parameters and change them as shown below:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5657b9ba-2980-4ecc-a772-a8c5642c99c3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[data_file_directories:
- /data/data
commitlog_directory: /logs/commitlog
hints_directory: /data/hints
saved_caches_directory: /data/saved_caches]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li data-uuid="88afb16f-0b86-460d-b36e-106ea67fe937"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Start Apache Cassandra by executing the following command:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="489be1f4-0a8b-4c6e-b865-bed3f911251a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo systemctl start cassandra]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li data-uuid="645ff482-8424-4bd0-8a4a-baca30a0c83d"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Check if Apache Cassandra is running by executing the following command:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="592c72ee-1452-4673-b3d6-1db92d1e6336"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[nodetool status]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">If Apache Cassandra is running, you should receive the output displayed below. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If the service is fully operational, the first 2 characters of the last line are &quot;</span>UN<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">&quot;, indicating that the node status is </span>U<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">p, and its state is </span>N<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">ormal.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dec272aa-e4fa-47a2-80ca-5fb891e9ae13"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Datacenter: datacenter1
=======================
Status=Up/Down
|/ State=Normal/Leaving/Joining/Moving
--  Address    Load       Tokens   Owns (effective)  Host ID                               Rack
UN  127.0.0.1  128.4 KB   256      100.0%            ea3f99eb-c4ad-4d13-95a1-80aec71b750f  rack1]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Configuring Cassandra memory usage</h3><p class="auto-cursor-target">If you did not use the installation script or want to increase the RAM usage by Cassandra, make the following changes. Otherwise, these configuration changes are set automatically by the Cassandra installation script.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac888467-01d1-4c59-93d5-2ac616dfa2fd"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Set the maximum direct memory size to ensure your off-heap structures (like memtables and caches) have a dedicated, stable pool of memory and prevent the node from crashing due to memory limits.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To configure the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">maximum direct memory size</span>, you must explicitly set the standard JVM argument -XX:MaxDirectMemorySize, in the appropriate JVM options file jvmXX-server.options.</p><ul><li data-uuid="293a9c8d-1d7a-40e5-838f-576bfcac7beb">Default file location: /etc/cassandra/conf/jvmXX-server.options</li><li data-uuid="461b9f43-697e-4a49-8a06-e38f5ef8e80f">You may need to add the -XX:MaxDirectMemorySize line if it is missing, or edit the existing one.</li><li data-uuid="bbf6f3b6-6efe-4fd5-933d-78e71ff14cd8">Replace jvmXX with your installed Java version (read requirements for the deployed version).</li></ul><p><strong>Edit jvmXX-server.options and add the line:</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5af2ed00-a0cb-467e-b32a-992b154fc02a"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-XX:MaxDirectMemorySize=10G]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To configure the Java Heap space for Cassandra, please see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Configuring Java heap space" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Configuring Java heap space]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><h3>Configuring Cassandra CPU usage</h3><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target" data-uuid="f697303b-d042-434f-90b5-1a0af08e14e1">In the jvmXX-server.options file, uncomment the following lines and set the values to the physical CPU core count (the values of both parameters should be the same):</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="655e3936-4277-4fee-aa05-fccf7d45373d"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-XX:ParallelGCThreads=8
-XX:ConcGCThreads=8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Additional configuration</h3><ul><li data-uuid="0e7c930e-e56d-44e0-bdc5-6729c5e1f75a">Synchronize CPU clocks on all Cassandra cluster nodes. Otherwise, you may encounter issues when creating an empty Cassandra cluster.</li><li data-uuid="ef09a0a1-39fe-4242-9f0a-0fe1acde38a2">When using <strong>cqlsh</strong>, the Python version required for cqlsh to work with Cassandra 5.0 is Python 3.8 to 3.11.</li><li data-uuid="432e0c0d-7b83-4b14-8e6e-bb3a64735bb3">In the logback.xml file, comment the &quot;&lt;appender-ref ref=&quot;ASYNCDEBUGLOG&quot; /&gt;&quot; line. This will increase Cassandra's performance by disabling the debug log.</li></ul><h3>Configuring Linux environment for Cassandra performance</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fcce1a17-001e-4c1b-9e30-ef82b8f277d8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you install <span>Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud </span>using the <em>install_twc_mcs_centos_rhel.sh</em> script, Cassandra performance is tuned automatically. However, if you plan to use other installation options or if you need to set other parameters after running the script, you can do it manually as described in this section.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To improve Apache Cassandra performance</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="fbbe9b37-75a9-4cd3-ba60-3c4d8d0ac5c6"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Locate the <em>sysctl.conf</em> file (default: /etc/sysctl.conf) and open it for editing.</p></li><li data-uuid="d03e5f24-62d9-410a-9622-91cd8fd0d7f7"><p class="auto-cursor-target">To configure the TCP settings, add the following tuning parameters to the file:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="00737da0-3dba-4b4c-9e5b-dd7b56cba87d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[net.core.rmem_max=16777216
net.core.wmem_max=16777216
net.core.optmem_max=40960
net.core.default_qdisc=fq
net.core.somaxconn=4096
net.ipv4.conf.all.arp_notify = 1
net.ipv4.tcp_keepalive_time=60
net.ipv4.tcp_keepalive_probes=3
net.ipv4.tcp_keepalive_intvl=10
net.ipv4.tcp_mtu_probing=1
net.ipv4.tcp_rmem=4096 12582912 16777216 
net.ipv4.tcp_wmem=4096 12582912 16777216 
net.ipv4.tcp_max_syn_backlog=8096
net.ipv4.tcp_slow_start_after_idle = 0
net.ipv4.tcp_tw_reuse = 1 
vm.max_map_count = 1048575
vm.swappiness = 0
vm.dirty_background_ratio=5
vm.dirty_ratio=80
vm.dirty_expire_centisecs = 12000]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li data-uuid="4f557284-1f2d-4103-90f9-5a4b4338934a"><p class="auto-cursor-target">To apply the setting without rebooting, execute the following command:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="401860c9-24af-44d0-b43a-0d1df77494d2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo sysctl -p]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>For more information about tuning Linux, see <a href="https://docs.datastax.com/en/dse/6.9/managing/configure/recommended-settings.html">DSE 6.9 Administrator Guide</a>.</p><h4><span>Using jemalloc memory allocator</span></h4><p><span>The jemalloc memory allocator package can potentially improve Cassandra performance. Our installation script does not install jemalloc. The easiest way to install this optional package is to first install the epel-release package. You can then pull the latest jemalloc release from the EPEL repository. An older version jemalloc is available for direct download and installation.</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="6fa29033-f7f5-4bf8-ae0f-a8394e40cfc0">EPEL package (optional, for pulling jemalloc package)</li></ul><ul><ul><li data-uuid="768df8f4-2716-4c43-a962-12c1ddd94dd0">Repository pull (epel-release)</li><li data-uuid="8ba6f257-471c-46dd-8b83-145226a6cbb9"><a href="https://dl.fedoraproject.org/pub/epel/epel-release-latest-8.noarch.rpm">RHEL 8 direct download</a></li><li data-uuid="8a9e82b3-50e4-4de5-a413-ff35ac1bfb0e"><a href="https://dl.fedoraproject.org/pub/epel/epel-release-latest-9.noarch.rpm">RHEL 9 direct download</a></li></ul></ul><ul><li data-uuid="8899ae07-765b-4a6a-927e-0140901dc0c2">jemalloc package (optional for performance)</li></ul><ul><ul><li data-uuid="90f418f6-8230-42ae-8084-c00c153899c8">EPEL Repository pull for latest version (jemalloc)</li><li data-uuid="314a3682-8e95-46ed-ae0a-8f85ad0791a0"><a href="https://repo.percona.com/yum/release/8/RPMS/x86_64/jemalloc-3.6.0-1.el8.x86_64.rpm">Older version (3.6) for direct download</a></li></ul></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047607 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00729: Installing Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin

- page_id: `247047607`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047607/Installing+Cameo+Collaborator+Publisher+plugin
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T15:01:41.438+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Publishing documents
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To publish models to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, install the Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin in your modeling tool.

#### WARNING: Compatibility

Compatibility

- The Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin is only compatible with the same version of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud.
- Make sure the Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin version is compatible with the version of your modeling tool. See the compatibility table .

For information regarding installation, licensing, and system requirements, visit the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installation, licensing, and system requirements' space='IL']page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To publish models to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, install the Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin in your modeling tool.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e30a40f-77ee-403b-876b-18c68c81d94f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Compatibility</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin is only compatible with the same version of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud.</li><li>Make sure the Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin version is compatible with the version of your modeling tool. See the <a href="https://www.nomagic.com/support/compatibility#Cameo%20Collaborator%20Publisher%20plugin">compatibility table</a>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">For information regarding installation, licensing, and system requirements, visit the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="Installation, licensing, and system requirements" /></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> page.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046474 space=MCS version=12 -->
## PAGE 00730: Installing Cassandra on Windows

- page_id: `247046474`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046474/Installing+Cassandra+on+Windows
- version_number: 12
- version_date: `2026-06-23T20:56:19.918+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Installation > Installation on Windows
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Downloads**

**[ATTACHMENT filename='CustomTWC_Cassandra5_DockerImg.zip']**

#### WARNING: Important! Cassandra is not supported on Windows!

Important! Cassandra is not supported on Windows!

We do not provide support for Cassandra installations on Windows as those installations are not natively supported by Apache. **We strongly recommend using Linux deployments.**The information below is provided as a courtesy only.

Warning:

development

Microsoft WSL documentation (Who is WSL for?)

One option for running Cassandra 5.0 on Windows is Docker. See [Install Docker Desktop on Windows](https://docs.docker.com/desktop/install/windows-install/) for official installation instructions.

Additional resources:

- https://hub.docker.com/_/cassandra/ – official Cassandra Docker image
- https://docs.docker.com/ – Docker documentation

##### Docker Cassandra basics

This section provides the basics for starting a Docker Cassandra 5.0 container, for testing purposes only. For production deployment, we recommend generating a custom Docker image as described in the section .

Start an instance of the official Cassandra 5.0.5 Docker image by executing this command:

```bash

```

| Parameter | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| --name | cassandra5_test | User-defined container instance name. This is an optional parameter. The name must be unique and cannot be the same as the official Docker image name. |
| -v | e:/cassandra/data/:/var/lib/cassandra | Mount an external drive to a path in the Docker container instance. /var/lib/cassandra is the default Cassandra path for data and commit log storage. An external drive must be mounted for permanent data storage. |
| -d |  | Detach and run in the background. |
| -p | 9042:9042 | Publish port 9042 to Windows host. 9042 is the native client port. |
| [image name]:version | cassandra:5.0.5 | Pull the official Apache Cassandra Docker image for this running instance. |

If this is a new Docker installation, the first **docker run** execution downloads the Cassandra image to the local system.

To access logs:

```bash
]]>
```

To access nodetool (or other tools):

```bash
nodetool status]]>
```

Newer Java versions require the **-Dcom.sun.jndi.rmiURLParsing=legacy** option. If the “Malformed IPv6 address” error occurs, use:

```bash
nodetool -Dcom.sun.jndi.rmiURLParsing=legacy status]]>
```

Docker instances are ephemeral by design. If an instance terminates or is deleted, all data, logs, and configuration files modified inside the instance are lost.

##### Custom Docker Cassandra image

Production deployment of Cassandra 5.0 on Windows requires modifying system configurations. Therefore, a custom Docker image must be created with the configuration changes. This section covers setting up and running a custom Docker image. Details of the configuration changes are discussed in section [Cassandra configuration](#InstallingCassandraonWindows-Cassandraconfiguration).

1. Download and extract the [ATTACHMENT filename='CustomTWC_Cassandra5_DockerImg.zip'] package into an empty working directory.
2. Execute the following command to build the custom Docker image named *cassandra5_twc*: bashDJangotrue
3. Start an instance of the custom Docker Cassandra 5.0 image you just built: bashDJangotrue The data and commit log directories are mounted separately, to different paths.

With the custom Docker Cassandra instance running and mounted to permanent storage, the database is ready to connect to your Teamwork Cloud installation.

If more configuration changes are needed, the running instance has to be terminated and a new Docker image needs to be built. Edit the configuration file from the working directory in Step 1. Proceed with steps 2-3 to redeploy the Cassandra node on Windows.

##### Cassandra configuration

This section is a summary of the configuration changes to optimize Docker Cassandra deployment for Teamwork Cloud. If additional configuration changes are needed, the Docker Cassandra image has to be rebuilt.

Synchronize the CPU clocks on all Cassandra cluster nodes. Otherwise, you may encounter issues when creating an empty Cassandra cluster.

Please see the [settings](https://docs.nomagic.com/MCS/magic-collaboration-studio/installing-apache-cassandra-247063421.html#id-InstallingApacheCassandra-ConfiguringApacheCassandraforfalseMagicCollaborationStudiomcs) to optimize Cassandra for better performance.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Cassandra is not supported on Windows!</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>We do not provide support for Cassandra installations on Windows as those installations are not natively supported by Apache. <strong>We strongly recommend using Linux deployments. </strong>The information below is provided as a courtesy only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df7aada5-3581-4274-ba07-e5d856db5ae4"><ac:rich-text-body><strong>Warning:</strong> Docker on Windows (including Docker Desktop and WSL) is intended for <strong>development </strong>purposes only. See <a href="https://docs.docker.com/desktop/install/windows-install/">Install Docker Desktop on Windows</a> for official installation instructions</span>.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb6a4db7-8911-4629-a4fd-65794d703b8f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Additional resources:</p><ul><li data-uuid="a492aa4f-448b-4d08-90f2-38891fd6e344"><a class="external-link" href="https://hub.docker.com/_/cassandra/">https://hub.docker.com/_/cassandra/</a><span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">–</span></span> official Cassandra Docker image</li><li data-uuid="2aef7570-4594-48d6-8551-d57bbd12de5d"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.docker.com/">https://docs.docker.com/</a><span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">–</span></span> Docker documentation</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Docker Cassandra basics</h3><p><span>This section provides the basics for starting a Docker Cassandra 5.0 container, for testing purposes only. For production deployment, we recommend generating a custom Docker image as described in the section <ac:link ac:anchor="Custom Docker Cassandra image" />.</span></p><p><span>Start an instance of the official Cassandra 5.0.5 Docker image by executing this command:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="93b36dcb-6670-47a7-b42a-201e8de1defb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker run --name cassandra5_test –p 9042:9042 –v e:\cassandra\data:/var/lib/cassandra -d cassandra:5.0.5]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup class=""> <col class="" /> <col class="" /> <col class="" /> </colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td><p><strong>Parameter</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Value</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>--name</p></td><td><p>cassandra5_test</p></td><td><p>User-defined container instance name. This is an optional parameter. The name must be unique and cannot be the same as the official Docker image name.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-v</p></td><td><p>e:/cassandra/data/:/var/lib/cassandra</p></td><td><p>Mount an external drive to a path in the Docker container instance. <em>/var/lib/cassandra</em> is the default Cassandra path for data and commit log storage. An external drive must be mounted for permanent data storage.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-d</p></td><td><br /></td><td><p>Detach and run in the background.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-p</p></td><td><p>9042:9042</p></td><td><p>Publish port 9042 to Windows host. 9042 is the native client port.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>[image name]:version</p></td><td><p>cassandra:5.0.5</p></td><td><p>Pull the official Apache Cassandra Docker image for this running instance.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="407abb9f-f047-4bde-b701-4c0df16c5e8c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If this is a new Docker installation, the first <strong>docker run</strong> execution downloads the Cassandra image to the local system.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>To access logs:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ee4f42b-5383-4f6c-8263-8ecdb77c87d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker logs <container name or ID>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>To access nodetool (or other tools):</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="61c9a14a-ca73-47d7-aba3-e4fcd7b5db55"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker exec -it <container name or ID> nodetool status]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>Newer Java versions require the <strong>-Dcom.sun.jndi.rmiURLParsing=legacy</strong> option. If the “Malformed IPv6 address” error occurs, use:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d819407-fb99-492e-b896-103aada6fb21"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker exec -it <container name or ID> nodetool -Dcom.sun.jndi.rmiURLParsing=legacy status]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5efaa53d-82fd-4b6e-9307-dd84497bfabc"><ac:rich-text-body>Docker instances are ephemeral by design. If an instance terminates or is deleted, all data, logs, and configuration files modified inside the instance are lost.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Custom Docker Cassandra image</h3><p><span>Production deployment of Cassandra 5.0 on Windows requires modifying system configurations. Therefore, a custom Docker image must be created with the configuration changes. This section covers setting up and running a custom Docker image. Details of the configuration changes are discussed in section <a href="#InstallingCassandraonWindows-Cassandraconfiguration">Cassandra configuration</a>.</span></p><ol><li data-uuid="800da558-5e23-4bb0-836b-8166f993c54b"><span>Download and extract the <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="CustomTWC_Cassandra5_DockerImg.zip" /></ac:link> package into an empty working directory.</span></li><li data-uuid="1f949a3f-6921-40d0-8dce-ab97985920a2"><p><span>Execute the following command to build the custom Docker image named <em>cassandra5_twc</em>:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="286a7979-36eb-4e4b-b28e-6e8a59974b1a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker build –t cassandra5_twc:latest .]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li data-uuid="60477adc-47e9-4bd4-98fd-cf11d2580282"><p><span>Start an instance of the custom Docker Cassandra 5.0 image you just built:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3904ca1e-101c-4e70-aa0f-15bb864a354f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker run –p 9042:9042 –v d:\data:/data –v e:\log\commitlog:/logs/commitlog -d cassandra5_twc:latest]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4106f393-3cdb-47a7-8d9c-657730c6316c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The data and commit log directories are mounted separately, to different paths.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><span>With the custom Docker Cassandra instance running and mounted to permanent storage, the database is ready to connect to your Teamwork Cloud installation.</span></p><p><span>If more configuration changes are needed, the running instance has to be terminated and a new Docker image needs to be built. Edit the configuration file from the working directory in Step 1. Proceed with steps 2-3 to redeploy the Cassandra node on Windows.</span></p><h3>Cassandra configuration</h3><p><span>This section is a summary of the configuration changes to optimize Docker Cassandra deployment for Teamwork Cloud. If additional configuration changes are needed, the Docker Cassandra image has to be rebuilt.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="44bae19f-b953-4a3b-92fe-e38c5a186636"><ac:rich-text-body>Synchronize the CPU clocks on all Cassandra cluster nodes. Otherwise, you may encounter issues when creating an empty Cassandra cluster.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>Please see the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MCS/magic-collaboration-studio/installing-apache-cassandra-247063421.html#id-InstallingApacheCassandra-ConfiguringApacheCassandraforfalseMagicCollaborationStudiomcs">settings</a> to optimize Cassandra for better performance.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046817 space=MCS version=3 -->
## PAGE 00731: Installing Microsoft TrueType fonts for Document Exporter

- page_id: `247046817`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046817/Installing+Microsoft+TrueType+fonts+for+Document+Exporter
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-10-09T13:06:45.054+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Administration Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Document Exporter for Cameo Collaborator requires Microsoft TrueType fonts to be installed on the server. Otherwise, PDF/HTML document export will fail. Alternative font packages, such as dejavu-serif-fonts, provide support for TrueType fonts and will work with Document Exporter. However, only Microsoft TrueType font will guarantee that the exported document retains original document formatting.

##### Prerequisites

- EPEL package repository must be installed and enabled.
- cabextract package must be installed from EPEL repo.

##### 1General method for installing font package

The steps provided here describes a universal method for installing the Microsoft TrueType font package on a Linux system. An online staging environment is required if installing on an air-gapped system.

To install the Microsoft TrueType font package:

1. D ownload font installer package to a predefined working path and extract : bashDJangotrue
2. Verify font extraction and generate the font package for installation : bashDJangotrue
3. If installing to an air-gapped environment, copy font package to the Document Exporter server.
4. Install fonts to the server and verify installation: bashDJangotrue
5. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform' space=''].

##### 2Online RPM installation method for RHEL 8

To install the Microsoft TrueType fonts directly from online RPM package on a Red Hat 8 system:

1. I nstall Microsoft TrueType Core RPM package : bashDJangotrue
2. After installation, use the fc-list command to verify installation: bashDJangotrue]]>
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform' space=''] .

The URLs used in the commands above are subject to change. Always make sure that you install the latest versions of fonts.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Document Exporter for Cameo Collaborator requires Microsoft TrueType fonts to be installed on the server. Otherwise, PDF/HTML document export will fail. Alternative font packages, such as dejavu-serif-fonts, provide support for TrueType fonts and will work with Document Exporter. However, only Microsoft TrueType font will guarantee that the exported document retains original document formatting. </p><h3><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: -0.003em;">Prerequisites</span></h3><ul style="text-align: left;"><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">EPEL package repository must be installed and enabled.</span></li><li>cabextract package must be installed from EPEL repo.</li></ul><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0140651e-9b15-4488-8686-3edb9dc3dd4f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>General method for installing font package</h3><p>The steps provided here describes a universal method for installing the Microsoft TrueType font package on a Linux system. An online staging environment is required if installing on an air-gapped system.</p><p>To install the Microsoft TrueType font package:</p><hr /><ol><li>D<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">ownload font installer package to a predefined working path and extract</span>:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9b0b645d-338b-496e-9bac-59865cd8e94f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[WORK_PATH=/opt/local/install/temp
sudo mkdir -p $WORK_PATH && cd $WORK_PATH
wget  https://phoenixnap.dl.sourceforge.net/project/mscorefonts2/sources/msttcore-fonts-installer-2.6.tar.gz
tar --strip-components=4 -xzf msttcore-fonts-installer-2.6.tar.gz -C $WORK_PATH msttcore-fonts-installer-2.6/usr/lib/msttcore-fonts-installer/
sudo mkdir -p $WORK_PATH/msttcore-fonts
./refresh-msttcore-fonts.sh -d $WORK_PATH/msttcore-fonts]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Verify font extraction and generate the font package for installation</span>:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="546828d6-6047-4b81-a3a1-9bc1ad32b9a3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ls -la $WORK_PATH/*.ttf
sudo pushd $WORK_PATH/msttcore-fonts && tar -cvzf $WORK_PATH/msttcore-fonts.tar.gz *.ttf && popd]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If installing to an air-gapped environment, copy font package to the Document Exporter server.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Install fonts to the server and verify installation:<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d880eeab-42d5-4523-a302-af9e711b9bcf"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo mkdir -p /usr/share/fonts/msttcore
tar -xzf msttcore-fonts.tar.gz -C /usr/share/fonts/msttcore/
ls -la /usr/share/fonts/msttcore
sudo fc-cache -fv]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Restart Web Application Platform]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b7c06111-135e-424e-8cb9-b927f826f6a5"><ac:parameter ac:name="">2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Online RPM installation method for RHEL 8</h3><p>To install the Microsoft TrueType fonts directly from online RPM package on a Red Hat 8 system:</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="268cdad5-1deb-44a7-9624-7c425e8df054">I</ac:inline-comment-marker> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="268cdad5-1deb-44a7-9624-7c425e8df054">nstall Microsoft TrueType Core RPM package</ac:inline-comment-marker>:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ece21b70-12f9-45a6-9e5f-9baea42dc6f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo rpm -i https://downloads.sourceforge.net/project/mscorefonts2/rpms/msttcore-fonts-installer-2.6-1.noarch.rpm]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>After installation, use the fc-list command to verify installation:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30d71a4d-7344-4a0b-862d-146e3ad948d2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[fc-list | grep -i <font name>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Restart Web Application Platform]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5f063d9f-9e13-4819-8183-c6435ae097df"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The URLs used in the commands above are subject to change. Always make sure that you install the latest versions of fonts.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046464 space=MCS version=23 -->
## PAGE 00732: Installing services

- page_id: `247046464`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046464/Installing+services
- version_number: 23
- version_date: `2026-04-09T10:43:57.411+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Installation > Installation on Linux
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The installation of product services and initial configuration is described in this section. Installation using the provided script is highly recommended. The process involves installing the service(s), generating an initial self-signed SSL certificate, creating the twcloud user for file and service ownership, then configuring the Linux environment for performance and security.

For a complete list of service files, please see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Web Application Platform services' space=''].

##### Prerequisites

- OpenJDK Java (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Third-Party Component Compatibility' space=''] for version information)

##### Installing product services

To install product services

1. Install Java from [Adoptium](https://adoptium.net/temurin/releases). As an alternative, the OpenJDK Java provided in the base Linux package repository can be used: bashDJangotrue-openjdk]]>
2. Download and extract the installation file from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL'] (< product_name>_<version_number>_installer_linux64.bin ). Place the installation file and script in an executable directory.
3. Install Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloudby executing the *[ATTACHMENT filename='install_twc_mcs_ol_rhel.sh']* script. bashDJangotrue
4. Follow the on-screen installation guidance.
5. When the Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloudpre-installation summary is displayed, check if the information is correct and press **ENTER.**
6. After installation is complete, optionally setup shortcuts and utilities for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Admin Tools' space=''] .
7. If Admin Tools was set up, execute *twc-start.sh* to start all services. Otherwise, manually start twcloud, zookeeper, and webapp services with the command: bashDJangotrue
8. Make sure that Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloudis operational by going to *https://<server_address>:8443/webapp.* You should see the login screen. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Accessing web applications' space=''].

When you complete the steps above, the preliminary product configuration is created. This means that your system is functional and you can log in to Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud. Refer to the section below for additional configurations and troubleshooting.

##### Additional configuration and troubleshooting

| Topic | Reference |
| --- | --- |
| Remote Cassandra node | In application.conf (shortcut: conf-twc), set the contact-points parameter to Cassandra node address. |
| Multi-server deployment | See . |
| System Error displayed on web browser | See . |
| Change SSL certificate | See . |
| Update Administrator password | See . |

###### Document exporter font package

To use Cameo Collaborator document exporter, a Microsoft TrueType font package is required in order to generate PDF/HTML files. Please see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing Microsoft TrueType fonts for Document Exporter' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

