Otherwise, manually start twcloud, zookeeper, and webapp services with the command: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ee20ace7-8bc6-4e88-9173-78e241eb1d0d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo systemctl start twcloud zookeeper webapp]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Make sure that <span>Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud </span>is operational by going to <em>https://&lt;server_address&gt;:8443/webapp.</em> You should see the login screen.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60704efe-55f4-467e-9f63-8e3e94932cf5"><ac:rich-text-body>For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Accessing web applications" /></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p>When you complete the steps above, the preliminary product configuration is created. This means that your system is functional and you can log in to <span>Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud</span>. Refer to the section below for additional configurations and troubleshooting.</p><h3>Additional configuration and troubleshooting</h3><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 94.9204%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 27.4969%;" /><col style="width: 72.5031%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Topic</th><th>Reference</th></tr><tr><td>Remote Cassandra node</td><td>In <em>application.conf</em> (shortcut: <em>conf-twc</em>), set the <em>contact-points</em> parameter to Cassandra node address.</td></tr><tr><td>Multi-server deployment</td><td>See <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Advanced services configuration" /></ac:link>.</td></tr><tr><td>System Error displayed on web browser</td><td>See <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="FAQ and Troubleshooting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[FAQ entry]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</td></tr><tr><td>Change SSL certificate</td><td>See <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing SSL certificate" /></ac:link>.</td></tr><tr><td>Update Administrator password</td><td>See <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Configuring or changing Administrator user password" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Setting up Administrator user]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>Document exporter font package</h4><p>To use Cameo Collaborator document exporter, a Microsoft TrueType font package is required in order to generate PDF/HTML files. Please see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Installing Microsoft TrueType fonts for Document Exporter" /></ac:link>. </p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046486 space=MCS version=7 -->
## PAGE 00733: Installing services on Windows

- page_id: `247046486`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046486/Installing+services+on+Windows
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2026-02-10T13:36:42.193+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Installation > Installation on Windows
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section provides instructions on installing Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud using either the installer or the zip file. 
Once the installation is complete, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying the license' space=''] before starting to use the product with the modeling tool and Teamwork Cloud Admin.

**IMPORTANT**. To use modeling tools with DSLS licenses,Microsoft Visual C++ Redistributable must be installed (see[Latest supported Visual C++ Redistributable downloads](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/windows/latest-supported-vc-redist?view=msvc-170#visual-studio-2015-2017-2019-and-2022)). This applies to Windows OS only.

The following sections provide instructions on how to install Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloudon Windows using the installer or the zip file.

For a complete list of service files, please see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Web Application Platform services' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This section provides instructions on installing <span>Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud</span> using either the installer or the zip file. <br />Once the installation is complete, you need to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying the license" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[apply the license]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> before starting to use the product with the modeling tool and Teamwork Cloud <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="22174434-dbe2-421c-a044-d6d784b16392">Admin</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><strong>IMPORTANT</strong></span>. To use modeling tools with DSLS licenses, </span><span>Microsoft Visual C++ Redistributable must be installed (see </span><a class="external-link" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/windows/latest-supported-vc-redist?view=msvc-170#visual-studio-2015-2017-2019-and-2022">Latest supported Visual C++ Redistributable downloads</a><span>). This applies to Windows OS only.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The following sections provide instructions on how to install <span>Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud </span>on Windows using the installer or the zip file.</span></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="06462469-5b89-42ec-9cf9-2e2d6212f709" /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="19e84c52-58cc-4134-a96f-fd05ce8d7ab0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For a complete list of service files, please see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Web Application Platform services" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046462 space=MCS version=9 -->
## PAGE 00734: Installing the FlexNet server

- page_id: `247046462`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046462/Installing+the+FlexNet+server
- version_number: 9
- version_date: `2026-03-06T14:35:39.272+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Installation > Installation on Linux
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud can be configured to access a FlexNet server for license checkout and validation. The FlexNet server can be installed on the same system or on a separate machine.

##### Prerequisites

- OpenJDK Java (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Third-Party Component Compatibility' space=''] for version information)
- Linux utility package unzip (repository)
- [FlexNet 11.19.8 64-bit: lmadmin installer](https://d1oqhepk9od1tu.cloudfront.net/Flex_License_Server_Utilities/v11.19.8/linux64/lmadmin-x64_linux-11_19_8_0.bin)
- [FlexNet 11.19.8 64-bit utilities: lmgrd (zip file)](https://d1oqhepk9od1tu.cloudfront.net/Flex_License_Server_Utilities/v11.19.8/linux64/lmgrd.zip)
- [Cameo daemon for FlexNet 11.19.8 64-bit (zip file)](https://d1oqhepk9od1tu.cloudfront.net/Flex_License_Server_Utilities/v11.19.8/linux64/cameo.zip)

##### Installing FlexNet server

The *install_flex_ol_rhel.sh* script will download all required components, deploy the server, create the systemd service entry to control it, and create the necessary firewall rules to allow the required traffic (if firewalld is running). The script may require modification depending on the zone where the interface is located. In addition, the script creates the lmadmin user, which runs the lmadmin service.

The script can also be used for offline installation. Download and place the FlexNet and Cameo daemon packages in the same location as the script. The lmadmin installer can work with several versions of Java, but at least one version needs to be installed on the system in order for the installer to run.

To install the FlexNet server

1. Run the *install_flex_ol_rhel.sh* script to start the installation and follow the installer instructions. All default values presented by the installer should be accepted, except for the installation path, which may be customized if you do not choose the silent installation.
2. After the lmadmin service is installed, start it by executing the following command: bashDJangotrue
3. To check if the service is running, execute the following command: bashDJangotrue

##### Troubleshooting

If the Imadmin service failed to start, it is often because the built-in web server cannot resolve the hostname.

To check if this is the case, execute the following commands:

```bash
/FNPLicenseServerManager/logs
tail web.log]]>
```

You will see output similar to the following, where *yourhostname* is the name of the host:

```bash

```

If this is the case, edit the */etc/hosts* file and add the following entry so the web server can resolve the host:

```bash

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

and follow the installer instructions.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="46feebe0-02c6-4b8b-b000-1aefc549fc8b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span the hostname.</p><p>To check if this is the case, execute the following commands:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f9a49d3-00f0-417c-b792-395cae3f8739"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd <install_root>/FNPLicenseServerManager/logs
tail web.log]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You will see output similar to the following, where <em>yourhostname</em> is the name of the host:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ac3268d-3c04-400c-9055-a12cd1d530f2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[[Tue May 02 18:43:27] [alert] (EAI 2)Name or service not known:
mod_unique_id: unable to find IPv4 address of "yourhostname"
Configuration Failed]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>If this is the case, edit the <em>/etc/hosts</em> file and add the following entry so the web server can resolve the host:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ead0927b-5a60-4189-9887-25f891abc2e2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[192.168.130.10  yourhostname]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314183006 space=MCS version=18 -->
## PAGE 00735: Installing using the zip file on linux

- page_id: `314183006`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/314183006/Installing+using+the+zip+file+on+linux
- version_number: 18
- version_date: `2026-07-13T22:50:53.459+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Installation > Installation on Linux
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section explains how to install Teamwork Cloud on a single server by using zip files and setting up its configuration file to allow access to the authentication server.

##### Prerequisites

- OpenJDK Java (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Third-Party Component Compatibility' space=''] for version information)

##### Installing Teamwork Cloud on Linux by unzipping the file

To install Teamwork Cloud on Linux by unzipping the file

1. Unzip the contents of the TeamworkCloudSuite directory to the installation directory (for example, <install_root> \TeamworkCloud ).
2. Unzip the contents of WebServiceWarFiles<version>.zip to the webapps directory (for example, <install_root>\WebAppPlatform/webapps/ ).
3. Open the server configuration file *<install_root>**\TeamworkCloud\configuration\application.conf* and edit the following lines (the hostname and seed-nodes) to use the node's own IP address. 
You must also set the IP address of the Cassandra node in the seeds section. If your Cassandra instance is on the same server, then use the node's own IP address. hostname = "10.1.1.123" seed-nodes = ["akka://twcloud@10.1.1.123:2552"] text contact-points = ["10.1.1.123:9042"] text
4. Update the AuthServer configuration: *<install_root>\WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\authserver.properties* by specifying the IP address instead of *<serverIP>*: text:8443>/
twc.server.host=]]>
5. Update the configuration file: *<install_root>* \WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\webappplatform.properties - the configuration file for Teamwork Cloud Admin Console
  - twc.admin.username - Set it to the username of a local account with Administrator privileges (default is Administrator)
  - twc.admin.password - Set it to the password corresponding to the Administrator user (default is Administrator).
  - If you changed the pswd field in <install_root> \TeamworkCloud\configuration\application.conf from the default, you must modify this file accordingly. Search for authentication.client.secret .and replace the CHANGE_ME value with the same value as that in application.conf .
6. Generate default keypair and certificate by running the following command from the command prompt. If your TeamworkCloudSuite is under a folder that requires Administrative permission, for example, ***/opt/local***, make sure that you run it with elevated permissions bashDJangotrue

```text
authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist=https://<serverIP>:8443>/webapp/,https://<PublicIP>:8443>/webapp/,https://<FQDN>:8443>/webapp/,https://md_redirect/
```

There are additional steps to run Teamwork Cloud as a service on Linux. Before proceeding, make sure the server's log configuration points to the file location that is writable. You can find the log config in the file*<install_root>\TeamworkCloud\configuration\logback.xml*.

You need to check if the file location path is editable.

##### Running Teamwork Cloud on Linux

To run Teamwork Cloud on Linux

1. Ensure that JAVA is set to a valid directory containing a Java JDK or JRE, if it is not already configured. For example,

```shell

```

2. Run the Teamwork Cloud service installation script, for example:

```text

```

3. Run the WebApp and the Zookeeper service installation script, for example:

```shell

```

4. Set service dependencies:

Run

```shell

```

Add the following to the override file

```shell

```

Reload the systemd manager configuration.

```shell

```

- Run the installation scripts as the root user or with sudo .
- If you modify the Teamwork Cloud environment configuration (for example, /etc/twcloud/twcloud-env ) or the systemd service definition, restart the service for the changes to take effect. If the systemd unit file was modified, run systemctl daemon-reload before restarting the service. Reinstallation of the service is not normally required

##### Uninstalling Teamwork Cloud on Linux

To uninstall Teamwork Cloud:

- Run the service uninstallation scripts, for example:

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This section explains how to install Teamwork Cloud on a single server by using zip files and setting up its configuration file to allow access to the authentication server.</p><h3><span>Prerequisites</span></h3><ul><li data-uuid="57a4a619-e0c7-47ad-b8ea-06109d7f9f29">OpenJDK Java (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Third-Party Component Compatibility" /></ac:link> for version information)</li></ul><h3>Installing Teamwork Cloud on Linux by unzipping the file</h3><p><span>To install Teamwork Cloud on Linux by unzipping the file</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="8a064b5c-de51-4fad-8715-e1a6b0bef69b"><span>Unzip the contents of the TeamworkCloudSuite directory to the installation directory (for example, </span><em>&lt;install_root&gt;</em><em>\TeamworkCloud</em><span>).</span></li><li data-uuid="edd3cfe5-6a57-4ba3-993e-80a4fd903dd2">Unzip the contents of WebServiceWarFiles&lt;version&gt;.zip to the webapps directory (for example, <em>&lt;install_root&gt;\WebAppPlatform/webapps/</em>).</li><li data-uuid="be9f3d41-3713-4bb1-bfb2-973ad13fe60d"><p>Open the server configuration file <em>&lt;install_root&gt;</em><em>\TeamworkCloud\configuration\application.conf</em> and edit the following lines (the hostname and seed-nodes) to use the node's own IP address.<br />You must also set the IP address of the Cassandra node in the seeds section. If your Cassandra instance is on the same server, then use the node's own IP address.</p><pre>hostname = &quot;10.1.1.123&quot;</pre><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34871a18-c07b-42db-a7d0-28bfb2a2180b"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[### Configuration for the Netty based transport drivers
	artery.canonical {
 		# The hostname or ip clients should connect to.
 		# If it is not set, it will be set automatically to the IP that can connect to the seed node.
		hostname = "10.1.1.123"

		# The default remote server port clients should connect to.
      		# Default is 2552 (AKKA). Use 0 if you want a random available port.
      		# This port needs to be unique for each actor system on the same machine.
		port = 2552]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><pre>seed-nodes = [&quot;akka://twcloud@10.1.1.123:2552&quot;]</pre><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae0e24aa-ea78-43a6-a32f-1f92ab696836"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cluster {
		# Initial contact points of the cluster.
    		# The nodes to join automatically at startup.
    		# The comma separating full URIs defined by a String in the form of
    		# "akka://system@hostname:port"
		# Can be set by system property. 
		# e.g. ["akka://twcloud@10.1.1.123:2552","akka://twcloud@10.1.1.124:2552"]
		# The default value depends on the operation mode. In cluster mode, the seed node is mandatory in all nodes.
		# In single server mode, the seed node configuration is optional. It is set to 127.0.0.1 by default at runtime.
		seed-nodes = ["akka://twcloud@10.1.1.123:2552"]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><pre>contact-points = [&quot;10.1.1.123:9042&quot;]</pre><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="825587f7-3596-4c34-af4f-5496c97f1b45"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[esi.persistence.datastax-java-driver {
                basic {
                                contact-points = ["10.1.1.123:9042"]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="7927c0aa-dc52-4851-9c04-32f661cf1a69"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Update the AuthServer configuration: <em>&lt;install_root&gt;\WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\authserver.properties</em> by specifying the IP address instead of <em>&lt;serverIP&gt;</em>:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b7045396-9880-498a-9633-35a52e588e91"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist=https://<serverIP>:8443>/
twc.server.host=<serverIP>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span> </span></p></li><li data-uuid="08d7b137-c30f-4670-b220-9dbc08aad057"><span>Update the configuration file: <em>&lt;install_root&gt;</em></span><em>\WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\webappplatform.properties</em><strong> </strong><span>- the configuration file for Teamwork Cloud Admin Console</span><ul><li data-uuid="a1fbd646-5c06-44ba-99ec-acaf1bf1b12b"><strong>twc.admin.username</strong> - Set it to the username of a local account with Administrator privileges (default is Administrator)</li><li data-uuid="0eaf7dcc-44d7-431e-ae30-adde31b77029"><strong>twc.admin.password</strong> - Set it to the password corresponding to the Administrator user (default is Administrator).</li><li data-uuid="5e378adb-ee4d-447c-b037-6ccd2c52369c">If you changed the <strong>pswd</strong> field in <em>&lt;install_root&gt;</em><em>\TeamworkCloud\configuration\application.conf</em> from the default, you must modify this file accordingly. Search for <strong>authentication.client.secret</strong>.and replace the <strong>CHANGE_ME</strong> value with the same value as that in <strong>application.conf</strong>.</li></ul><p><br /></p></li><li data-uuid="73551088-6149-42c3-a574-e9f2e3061d7d"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Generate default keypair and certificate by running the following command from the command prompt. If your TeamworkCloudSuite is under a folder that requires Administrative permission, for example, <em><strong>/opt/local</strong></em>, make sure that you run it with elevated permissions</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="649529e7-97de-4dba-bfe0-5bd73df70e71"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd /opt/local/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/scripts/linux
./genkey]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eed5ef97-6f9e-4ef6-87f7-dbe5c059cd5d"><ac:rich-text-body>If the server is accessed via FQDN or public IP address, then the authentication.redirect.url must also contain entries for them, accordingly, in the format:<pre>authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist=https://&lt;serverIP&gt;:8443&gt;/webapp/,https://&lt;PublicIP&gt;:8443&gt;/webapp/,https://&lt;FQDN&gt;:8443&gt;/webapp/,<span class="nolink">https://md_redirect/</span></pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>There are additional steps to run Teamwork Cloud as a service on Linux. Before proceeding, make sure the server's log configuration points to the file location that is writable. Run the Teamwork Cloud service installation script, for example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62fe9187-48e8-4c35-8ba7-76181d1e7c41"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[/opt/local/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/scripts/linux/service.sh install]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">     3. Run the WebApp and the Zookeeper service installation script, for example:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9f7fc69-19c7-4bc6-8b5e-7eb99b1b361a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[/opt/local/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/bin/service.sh install 
/opt/local/CATIANoMagicServices/Zookeeper/bin/zkService.sh install]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>     4. Set service dependencies:</p><p>              Run </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a544b74e-b33c-4a5f-83c3-30f4aa17e2cb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[systemctl edit webapp.service]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>              Add the following to the override file</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd802a72-db83-425e-9dd2-8138898811e9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[[Unit]
Requires=twcloud.service zookeeper.service
After=twcloud.service zookeeper.service]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>             Reload the systemd manager configuration.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a12bffe-de4f-4ee3-84e3-3d01407f219c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[systemctl daemon-reload]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5bcc6b01-7045-4042-8cf1-e06fee51aa0a"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="ffa72bef-ad7c-46ab-a210-9ba1cbef87a2">Run the installation scripts as the <strong>root</strong> user or with <strong>sudo</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="873c2ff0-da41-4a68-b57a-9499adaab3b0">If you modify the Teamwork Cloud environment configuration (for example, <em>/etc/twcloud/twcloud-env</em>) or the systemd service definition, restart the service for the changes to take effect. If the systemd unit file was modified, run <em>systemctl daemon-reload</em> before restarting the service. Reinstallation of the service is not normally required</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Uninstalling Teamwork Cloud on Linux</h3><p>To uninstall Teamwork Cloud:</p><ul><li data-uuid="75df31db-0652-40ca-aabd-7952866e3164">Run the service uninstallation scripts, for example: </li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="503645fe-de8a-4ff3-9d39-75412d7221b3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[/opt/local/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/scripts/linux/service.sh remove
/opt/local/CATIANoMagicServices/Zookeeper/bin/zkService.sh remove
/opt/local/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/bin/service.sh remove]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046504 space=MCS version=9 -->
## PAGE 00736: Installing using the zip file on Windows

- page_id: `247046504`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046504/Installing+using+the+zip+file+on+Windows
- version_number: 9
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:00:06.198+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Installation > Installation on Windows > Installing services on Windows
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section explains how to install Teamwork Cloud on a single server by using zip files and setting up its configuration file to allow access to the authentication server.

##### Prerequisites

- OpenJDK Java (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Third-Party Component Compatibility' space=''] for version information)

##### Installing Teamwork Cloud on Windows by unzipping the file

To install Teamwork Cloud on Windows by unzipping the file

1. Unzip the contents of the TeamworkCloudSuite directory to the installation directory (for example, <install_root> \TeamworkCloud ).
2. Open the server configuration file *<install_root>**\TeamworkCloud\configuration\application.conf* and edit the following lines (the hostname and seed-nodes) to use the node's own IP address. 
You must also set the IP address of the Cassandra node in the seeds section. If your Cassandra instance is on the same server, then use the node's own IP address. hostname = "10.1.1.123" seed-nodes = ["akka://twcloud@10.1.1.123:2552"] text contact-points = ["10.1.1.123:9042"] text
3. Update the AuthServer configuration: *<install_root>\WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\authserver.properties* by specifying the IP address instead of *<serverIP>*: text:8443>/
twc.server.host=]]>
4. Update the configuration file: *<install_root>* \WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\webappplatform.properties - the configuration file for Teamwork Cloud Admin Console
  - twc.admin.username - Set it to the username of a local account with Administrator privileges (default is Administrator)
  - twc.admin.password - Set it to the password corresponding to the Administrator user (default is Administrator).
  - If you changed the pswd field in <install_root> \TeamworkCloud\configuration\application.conf from the default, you must modify this file accordingly. Search for authentication.client.secret .and replace the CHANGE_ME value with the same value as that in application.conf .
5. Generate default keypair and certificate by running the following command from the command prompt. If your TeamworkCloudSuite is under a folder that requires Administrative permission, for example, *C:\Program Files*, make sure that you start the command prompt using**"Run as administrator"**. bashDJangotruecd C:\Program Files\CATIANoMagicServices\TeamworkCloud\scripts\windows
C:\> genkey]]>

```text
authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist=https://<serverIP>:8443>/webapp/,https://<PublicIP>:8443>/webapp/,https://<FQDN>:8443>/webapp/,https://md_redirect/
```

There are additional steps to run Teamwork Cloud as a service on Windows. Before proceeding, make sure the server's log configuration points to the file location that is writable. You can find the log config in the file*<install_root>\TeamworkCloud\configuration\logback.xml*.

You need to check if the file location path is editable.

##### Running Teamwork Cloud on Windows

To run Teamwork Cloud on Windows

1. Ensure that JAVA_HOME is set to a valid directory containing a Java JDK or JRE, if it is not already configured. For example, shelltrue
2. Run the Teamwork Cloud service installation script, for example: textinstallService.bat]]>
3. Run the WebApp and the Zookeeper service installation script textservice.bat install 
C:\Program Files\CATIANoMagicServices\Zookeeper\bin\>zkService.cmd install]]>
4. Set service dependencies: bashDJangotruesc config WebApp depend= TeamworkCloud]]>

- The scripts should be executed from an elevated command prompt ( Run as Administrator ).
- On Windows, the values of the environment variables are evaluated only once, when the service is installed. Subsequent changes to these variables will not affect the service startup. Therefore, if the values are modified and the service must use the new values, the service must be re-installed.

##### Uninstalling Teamwork Cloud on Windows

To uninstall Teamwork Cloud:

- Run the service uninstallation scripts:

```text
uninstallService.bat 
C:\Program Files\CATIANoMagicServices\WebAppPlatform\bin\>service.bat uninstall 
C:\Program Files\CATIANoMagicServices\Zookeeper\bin\>zkService.cmd remove]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This section explains how to install Teamwork Cloud on a single server by using zip files and setting up its configuration file to allow access to the authentication server.</p><h3><span>Prerequisites</span></h3><ul><li data-uuid="b06b615c-ac69-4f6f-86d4-31bb3460bc92">OpenJDK Java (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Third-Party Component Compatibility" /></ac:link> for version information)</li></ul><h3>Installing Teamwork Cloud on Windows by unzipping the file</h3><p><span>To install Teamwork Cloud on Windows by unzipping the file</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="f1d37deb-bf50-49ef-97c1-bd2a59d0d63e"><span>Unzip the contents of the TeamworkCloudSuite directory to the installation directory (for example, </span><em>&lt;install_root&gt;</em><em>\TeamworkCloud</em><span>).</span></li><li data-uuid="09a8f87e-4f42-4318-a1c1-5de0089c6cc5"><p>Open the server configuration file <em>&lt;install_root&gt;</em><em>\TeamworkCloud\configuration\application.conf</em> and edit the following lines (the hostname and seed-nodes) to use the node's own IP address.<br />You must also set the IP address of the Cassandra node in the seeds section. If your Cassandra instance is on the same server, then use the node's own IP address.</p><pre>hostname = &quot;10.1.1.123&quot;</pre><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34871a18-c07b-42db-a7d0-28bfb2a2180b"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[### Configuration for the Netty based transport drivers
	artery.canonical {
 		# The hostname or ip clients should connect to.
 		# If it is not set, it will be set automatically to the IP that can connect to the seed node.
		hostname = "10.1.1.123"

		# The default remote server port clients should connect to.
      		# Default is 2552 (AKKA). Use 0 if you want a random available port.
      		# This port needs to be unique for each actor system on the same machine.
		port = 2552]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><pre>seed-nodes = [&quot;akka://twcloud@10.1.1.123:2552&quot;]</pre><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae0e24aa-ea78-43a6-a32f-1f92ab696836"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cluster {
		# Initial contact points of the cluster.
    		# The nodes to join automatically at startup.
    		# The comma separating full URIs defined by a String in the form of
    		# "akka://system@hostname:port"
		# Can be set by system property. 
		# e.g. ["akka://twcloud@10.1.1.123:2552","akka://twcloud@10.1.1.124:2552"]
		# The default value depends on the operation mode. In cluster mode, the seed node is mandatory in all nodes.
		# In single server mode, the seed node configuration is optional. It is set to 127.0.0.1 by default at runtime.
		seed-nodes = ["akka://twcloud@10.1.1.123:2552"]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><pre>contact-points = [&quot;10.1.1.123:9042&quot;]</pre><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="825587f7-3596-4c34-af4f-5496c97f1b45"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[esi.persistence.datastax-java-driver {
                basic {
                                contact-points = ["10.1.1.123:9042"]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="ef2e936c-5132-4e6e-b651-b038dd1260c7"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Update the AuthServer configuration: <em>&lt;install_root&gt;\WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\authserver.properties</em> by specifying the IP address instead of <em>&lt;serverIP&gt;</em>:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b7045396-9880-498a-9633-35a52e588e91"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist=https://<serverIP>:8443>/
twc.server.host=<serverIP>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span> </span></p></li><li data-uuid="d3203ee4-3ebf-4c6f-908e-202622d50c09"><span>Update the configuration file: <em>&lt;install_root&gt;</em></span><em>\WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\webappplatform.properties</em><strong> </strong><span>- the configuration file for Teamwork Cloud Admin Console</span><ul><li><strong>twc.admin.username</strong> - Set it to the username of a local account with Administrator privileges (default is Administrator)</li><li><strong>twc.admin.password</strong> - Set it to the password corresponding to the Administrator user (default is Administrator).</li><li>If you changed the <strong>pswd</strong> field in <em>&lt;install_root&gt;</em><em>\TeamworkCloud\configuration\application.conf</em> from the default, you must modify this file accordingly. Search for <strong>authentication.client.secret</strong>.and replace the <strong>CHANGE_ME</strong> value with the same value as that in <strong>application.conf</strong>.</li></ul><p><br /></p></li><li data-uuid="ba63c3e3-2ac2-4bf3-8f2a-eb9d7ceb3714"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Generate default keypair and certificate by running the following command from the command prompt. If your TeamworkCloudSuite is under a folder that requires Administrative permission, for example, <em>C:\Program Files</em>, make sure that you start the command prompt using<strong> &quot;Run as administrator&quot;</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="649529e7-97de-4dba-bfe0-5bd73df70e71"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\> cd C:\Program Files\CATIANoMagicServices\TeamworkCloud\scripts\windows
C:\> genkey]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eed5ef97-6f9e-4ef6-87f7-dbe5c059cd5d"><ac:rich-text-body>If the server is accessed via FQDN or public IP address, then the authentication.redirect.url must also contain entries for them, accordingly, in the format:<pre>authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist=https://&lt;serverIP&gt;:8443&gt;/webapp/,https://&lt;PublicIP&gt;:8443&gt;/webapp/,https://&lt;FQDN&gt;:8443&gt;/webapp/,<span class="nolink">https://md_redirect/</span></pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>There are additional steps to run Teamwork Cloud as a service on Windows. Before proceeding, make sure the server's log configuration points to the file location that is writable. You can find the log config in the file<em> &lt;install_root&gt;\TeamworkCloud\configuration\logback.xml</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="443c2fda-8806-4a26-b700-9b2dea6df368"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You need to check if the file location path is editable.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span>Running Teamwork Cloud on Windows<br /></span></h3><p><span>To run Teamwork Cloud on Windows  </span></p><p><span>        </span></p><ol><li data-uuid="444e5bdb-51cf-4e91-bdd4-66ed90f3c584">Ensure that JAVA_HOME is set to a valid directory containing a Java JDK or JRE, if it is not already configured. For example,<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ada33689-8005-4ce7-ac59-9a7f20803754"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[set JAVA_HOME=C:\Program Files\Eclipse Adoptium\jdk-21.0.10.7-hotspot]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="2f03d347-2231-40f7-b71d-a597a13c9f00"><p> Run the Teamwork Cloud service installation script, for example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62fe9187-48e8-4c35-8ba7-76181d1e7c41"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\Program Files\CATIANoMagicServices\TeamworkCloud\scripts\windows>installService.bat]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="484c2f58-20a5-44a8-9b77-25004894f147"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Run the WebApp and the Zookeeper service installation script</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="57e02c17-eea9-417c-8ab2-563a229392ba"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\Program Files\CATIANoMagicServices\WebAppPlatform\bin\>service.bat install 
C:\Program Files\CATIANoMagicServices\Zookeeper\bin\>zkService.cmd install]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="c3e907f1-75ec-4234-89c6-ab79cbff6139"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Set service dependencies:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca808acd-3a82-47e2-ae0e-e08390fa85ed"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\> sc config WebApp depend= TeamworkCloud]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5bcc6b01-7045-4042-8cf1-e06fee51aa0a"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="5cff9371-9d61-4172-8316-3b40f6921119">The scripts should be executed from an elevated command prompt (<strong>Run as Administrator</strong>).</li><li data-uuid="2764ed49-25fd-4a87-a071-f0d82d7d13ae">On Windows, the values of the environment variables are evaluated only once, when the service is installed. Subsequent changes to these variables will not affect the service startup. Therefore, if the values are modified and the service must use the new values, the service must be re-installed.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Uninstalling Teamwork Cloud on Windows</h3><p>To uninstall Teamwork Cloud:</p><ul><li data-uuid="027f58ef-d172-49d3-9390-dafcaf130b73">Run the service uninstallation scripts: </li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="503645fe-de8a-4ff3-9d39-75412d7221b3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\Program Files\CATIANoMagicServices\TeamworkCloud\scripts\windows>uninstallService.bat 
C:\Program Files\CATIANoMagicServices\WebAppPlatform\bin\>service.bat uninstall 
C:\Program Files\CATIANoMagicServices\Zookeeper\bin\>zkService.cmd remove]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046553 space=MCS version=22 -->
## PAGE 00737: Installing Web Application Platform manually

- page_id: `247046553`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046553/Installing+Web+Application+Platform+manually
- version_number: 22
- version_date: `2026-02-09T10:40:15.789+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Installation > Advanced installation > Web Application Platform Installation on a separate machine
- labels: ['installing-and-configuring', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The content of this chapter is intended for system administrators. Here, you can learn how to install and configure Web Application Platform and its services without using the installer file.

Web Application Platform, with its services, is bundled with Apache Tomcat (this application should work in similar environments such as GlassFish, WildFly, and Eclipse Jetty).

#### NOTE: Installation prerequisites and other important information

Installation prerequisites and other important information

Before installing Web Application Platform with services, you need to:

- Have a working instance of Teamwork Cloud.
- If you intend to install the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud service, make sure that [DSLS](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=dsls-installation-and-licensing&version=latest) or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing the FlexNet server' space=''] is installed beforehand.
- For deployment on Linux, increase the open file limit for the OS (or for Web Application Platform/Tomcat) to a minimum of 20 000 files.
- Have access to the keystore file with the same certificate that is used in Teamwork Cloud for authentication configuration.
- Extract the <product_name>_<version_number>_linux64.zip file.
- Extract the Web_Application_Platform_<version number>_war_files.zip file, which includes the Web Application Platform service files.

Before starting the installation, you should know the following:

- We recommend installing Web Application Platform and Teamwork Cloud on different machines.
- Before installing the Web Application Platform, ensure that you note down the following information: ZooKeeper, Cassandra, Teamwork Cloud, and the license server information (e.g., hostname and port), as well as the keystore file location (located in the <install_root>/Certs directory). You will need this data during the installation process.
- If you have an all-in-one deployment, then it is highly recommended to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform' space=''] on the machine where Teamwork Cloud is installed.

To install Web Application Platform

1. Open the <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties file for editing, read the comments with property descriptions, and specify the following:
  - Web Application Platform properties
  - Authentication server properties
  - Teamwork Cloud server properties
  - FlexNet/DSLS server properties
  - Platform service discovery-related properties
2. To configure the Authentication server ( authentication.war ), a part of the Web Application Platform, do the following:
  1. Copy the keystore file to the <install_root>/Certs/ directory
  2. Open for editing the <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/*authserver.properties* file, read the comments with property descriptions, and specify the following:
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) General parameters' space='']
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Keystore parameters' space='']
3. Edit the following files:
  - <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties
  - <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/authserver.properties
4. Copy all web applications (WAR files) to <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/webapps/ You can remove the WAR files of applications that will not be used. (Example: document-exporter.war, simulation.war...)
5. Install the webapp service and enable it to start automatically. For example: true

For information on how to start Web Application Platform, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform' space='']. You can access Web Application Platform by opening a web browser and navigating to *https://<domain_name>:<port>/webapp*.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046537 space=MCS version=14 -->
## PAGE 00738: Installing Web Application Platform on Linux

- page_id: `247046537`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046537/Installing+Web+Application+Platform+on+Linux
- version_number: 14
- version_date: `2025-12-10T10:30:38.272+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Installation > Advanced installation > Web Application Platform Installation on a separate machine
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The content of this chapter is intended for system administrators. Here, you can learn how to install and configure Web Application Platform with Cameo Collaborator and other services by using the command-line interface.

#### NOTE: Installation prerequisites and other important information

Installation prerequisites and other important information

Before installing Web Application Platform with services, you need to:

- Have a working instance of Teamwork Cloud.
- If you intend to install the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud service, make sure that [DSLS](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=dsls-installation-and-licensing&version=latest) or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing the FlexNet server' space=''] is installed beforehand.
- Download and extract the installation file from the 3DS media package (< product_name>_<version_number>_installer_linux64.bin ). Place the installation file and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installation on Linux' space=''] in an executable directory.
- Increase the open file limit for the OS (or for Web Application Platform/Tomcat) to a minimum of 20 000 files.
- Have access to the keystore file with the same certificate that is used in Teamwork Cloud for authentication configuration.

Before starting the installation, you should know the following:

- We recommend installing Web Application Platform and Teamwork Cloud on different machines.
- Before installing Web Application Platform, make sure to note down Zookeeper, Cassandra, Teamwork Cloud, and the license server information (e.g., hostname and port), as well as the keystore file location (you can find it in the <install_root>/Certs directory). You will need this data during installation.
- After installing Web Application Platform, it is recommended to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform' space=''] on the machine where Teamwork Cloud is installed.

To install Web Application Platform and services (advanced installation)

1. Open the terminal and go to the directory where the < product_name> _<version number>_installer_linux64.bin file and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installation on Linux' space=''] are stored.
2. Install Web Application Platform with services by executing the *install_twc_mcs_ol_rhel.sh* script. shellDJangotrue
3. When the introduction to Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud installation is displayed, press **Enter** to continue.
4. Select the Advanced installation option and press Enter . (Example: Type "2" and press Enter.)
5. Deselect Teamwork Cloud and press Enter . (Example: Type "1" and press Enter.) There should be only one ZooKeeper running for one deployment. You can choose to deploy on the Teamwork Cloud server or Web Application Platform server.
6. Specify the location where you want to install Web Application Platform Services:
  - Press Enter to use the default installation directory.
  - Enter the full path to the installation directory and press Enter .
7. Press Enter to install all available services, or type a comma-separated list of numbers to choose the services you need to install.
8. Follow the on-screen installation instructions.
9. When the pre-installation summary is displayed, check if properties are specified correctly, and press Enter to start the installation.
10. When Web Application Platform Services is successfully installed , press Enter .
11. If you want to start the WebApp service right after installation, type "2". Otherwise, press Enter .
12. If you installed the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud service, apply the Cameo Collaborator license.

Web Application Platform is now installed with the selected services. For information on how to start Web Application Platform, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform' space='']. To access Web Application Platform, open a web browser and go to *https://<domain_name>:<port>/webapp*.

**Scripts for upgrading Java and Tomcat in the Linux environment:**

- Java: [ATTACHMENT filename='upgrade_jdk_webapp.sh']
- Tomcat: [ATTACHMENT filename='upgrade_tomcat_webapp.sh']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The content of this chapter is intended for system administrators. Here, you can learn how to install and configure Web Application Platform with Cameo Collaborator and other services by using the command-line interface.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="23a637d8-0dc4-4e2f-8517-02e8093618f4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Installation prerequisites and other important information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before installing Web Application Platform with services, you need to:</p><ul><li>Have a working instance of Teamwork Cloud.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If you intend to install the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud service, make sure that <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=dsls-installation-and-licensing&amp;version=latest">DSLS</a> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Installing the FlexNet server" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[the FlexNet license server]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is installed beforehand.</span></li><li data-uuid="85b6b729-0627-4766-a424-27f6ee981de1">Download and extract the installation file from the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=downloading-installation-files&amp;version=latest">3DS media package</a> (&lt;<em>product_name&gt;_&lt;version_number&gt;_installer_linux64.bin</em>). Place the installation file and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Installation on Linux" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[script]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in an executable directory.</li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046667/Advanced+services+configuration#Advancedservicesconfiguration-IncreasingopenfilelimitonLinux">Increase the open file limit for the OS</a> (or for Web Application Platform/Tomcat) to a minimum of 20 000 files.</li><li>Have access to the keystore file with the same certificate that is used in Teamwork Cloud for authentication configuration.</li></ul><p>Before starting the installation, you should know the following:</p><ul><li>We recommend installing Web Application Platform and Teamwork Cloud on different machines.</li><li>Before installing Web Application Platform, make sure to note down Zookeeper, Cassandra, Teamwork Cloud, and the license server information (e.g., hostname and port), as well as the keystore file location (you can find it in the <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/Certs</em> directory). You will need this data during installation.</li><li>After installing Web Application Platform, it is recommended to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[stop the WebApp service]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the machine where Teamwork Cloud is installed.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To install Web Application Platform and services (advanced installation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the terminal and go to the directory where the &lt;<em>product_name&gt;</em>_&lt;version number&gt;_installer_linux64.bin file and<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Installation on Linux" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ script]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are stored.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Install Web Application Platform with services by executing the <em>install_twc_mcs_ol_rhel.sh</em> script.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd04ca64-bc0c-445a-a3db-a209f7d3a364"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo ./install_twc_mcs_ol_rhel.sh]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="f4e71659-01ae-4cfb-be76-0be4ede9ed18"><p class="auto-cursor-target">When the introduction to <span>Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud</span> installation is displayed, press <strong>Enter</strong> to continue.</p></li><li data-uuid="bd9fcaf6-b438-4756-877a-c456a1648f3c">Select the <strong>Advanced</strong> installation option and press <strong>Enter</strong>. (Example: Type &quot;2&quot; and press Enter.)</li><li data-uuid="cd05c2b1-d2ac-42eb-a6c5-68d4d8b3b7e2">Deselect Teamwork Cloud and press <strong>Enter</strong>. (Example: Type &quot;1&quot; and press Enter.)<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f6d059c8-542f-4919-83ad-8e29bdc1fe98"><ac:rich-text-body>There should be only one ZooKeeper running for one deployment. You can choose to deploy on the Teamwork Cloud server or Web Application Platform server.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify the location where you want to install Web Application Platform Services:</span><ul><li>Press <strong>Enter</strong> to use the default installation directory.</li><li>Enter the full path to the installation directory and press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="a83f21c4-f9a2-4b6f-844b-11b18b7eacfb">Press <strong>Enter</strong> to install all available services, or type a comma-separated list of numbers to choose the services you need to install.</li><li>Follow the on-screen installation instructions.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">When the pre-installation summary is displayed, check if properties are specified correctly, and</span> press <strong>Enter</strong> to start the installation.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">When Web Application Platform Services is successfully installed</span>, press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li><li>If you want to start the WebApp service right after installation, type &quot;2&quot;. Otherwise, press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If you installed the</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud service,</span> apply the Cameo Collaborator license.</li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Web Application Platform is now installed with the selected services. For information on how to start Web Application Platform, refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform" /></ac:link>. To access Web Application Platform, open a web browser and go to <em>https://&lt;domain_name&gt;:&lt;port&gt;/webapp</em>.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Scripts for upgrading Java and Tomcat in the Linux environment:</strong></p><ul><li>Java: <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="upgrade_jdk_webapp.sh" /></ac:link></li><li>Tomcat: <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="upgrade_tomcat_webapp.sh" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046543 space=MCS version=8 -->
## PAGE 00739: Installing Web Application Platform on Windows

- page_id: `247046543`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046543/Installing+Web+Application+Platform+on+Windows
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2025-12-03T14:30:02.267+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Installation > Advanced installation > Web Application Platform Installation on a separate machine
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The content of this chapter is intended for system administrators. Here, you can learn how to install and configure Web Application Platform with Cameo Collaborator and other services used with Teamwork Cloud.

#### NOTE: Installation prerequisites and other important information

Installation prerequisites and other important information

Before installing Web Application Platform with services, you need to:

- Have a working instance of Teamwork Cloud.
- If you intend to install the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud service, make sure that [DSLS](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=dsls-installation-and-licensing&version=latest) or the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing the FlexNet server' space=''] is installed beforehand.
- Download the *<product_name>_<version number>_installer_win64.exe* file .
- Have access to the KeyStore file with the same certificate that is used in Teamwork Cloud for authentication configuration.

Before starting the installation, you should know the following:

- We recommend installing Web Application Platform and Teamwork Cloud on different machines.
- Before installing Web Application Platform, make sure to note down Zookeeper, Cassandra, Teamwork Cloud, and the license server information (e.g., hostname and port), as well as the KeyStore file location (you can find it in the <install_root>\Certs directory). You will need this data during installation.
- After installing Web Application Platform, it is recommended to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform' space=''] on the machine where Teamwork Cloud is installed.

To install Web Application Platform and services (advanced installation)

1. Run the <product_name>_<version number>_installer_win64.exe file as an administrator to start the installation wizard.
2. Select the Advanced Install radio button and click Next .
3. Deselect Teamwork Cloud and press Next. There should be only one ZooKeeper running for one deployment. You can choose to deploy on the Teamwork Cloud server or Web Application Platform server.
4. Follow the on-screen instructions in the installation wizard.
5. When the Pre-Installation Summary is displayed, check if all the installation properties are specified correctly and click Install .
6. After successful installation, click Next .
7. If you want to start the WebApp service right after installation, check the Start Service(s) check box.
8. Click Done to close the installation wizard.
9. If you installed the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud service, apply the Cameo Collaborator license.

Web Application Platform is now installed with the selected services. For information on how to start Web Application Platform, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform' space='']. To access Web Application Platform, open a web browser and go to *https://<domain_name>:<port>/webapp*.

You will need this data during installation.</li><li>After installing Web Application Platform, it is recommended to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[stop the WebApp service]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the machine where Teamwork Cloud is installed.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To install Web Application Platform and services (advanced installation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Run the <em>&lt;product_name&gt;_&lt;version number&gt;_installer_win64.exe</em> file as an administrator to start the installation wizard.</li><li data-uuid="6c450a94-5852-40eb-8fac-a49f40becbf1">Select the <strong>Advanced Install</strong> radio button and click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="f2e42d2d-fba9-47b1-a5c5-56613702eee7">Deselect Teamwork Cloud and press <strong>Next.</strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e15d053a-a355-4cb0-a7a8-3d9ffc7a2c5e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>There should be only one ZooKeeper running for one deployment. You can choose to deploy on the Teamwork Cloud server or Web Application Platform server.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><strong><br /></strong></li><li data-uuid="aeb9385d-8e11-4153-86d7-28fdaea581fa">Follow the on-screen instructions in the installation wizard.</li><li data-uuid="0c7469a7-be55-410f-8c12-ad27a64284e6">When the Pre-Installation Summary is displayed, check if all the installation properties are specified correctly and click <strong>Install</strong>.</li><li>After successful installation, click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li>If you want to start the WebApp service right after installation, check the <strong>Start Service(s)</strong> check box.</li><li>Click <strong>Done</strong> to close the installation wizard.</li><li><span>If you installed the</span><span> Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud service, apply the Cameo Collaborator license.</span></li></ol><p><br />Web Application Platform is now installed with the selected services. For information on how to start Web Application Platform, refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform" /></ac:link>. To access Web Application Platform, open a web browser and go to <em>https://&lt;domain_name&gt;:&lt;port&gt;/webapp</em>.</p>
