While Docker Compose is used as the primary example for orchestration, the configuration is flexible and can be adapted to your infrastructure - either by combining services into a single Compose file or separating them into multiple files. Furthermore, users may opt to build services individually using docker build and manage them as standalone containers for a more granular setup</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Prerequisites</h3><p> For Teamwork Cloud requirements, see the official documentation here: <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/TWCloudTWRT/pages/127967291/System+requirements?src=contextnavpagetreemode">TWC Requirements</a></p><p> To install Docker, follow the official steps from <a href="https://docs.docker.com">Docker’s documentation</a></p><h3>Directory structure</h3><p>The following directory structure separates configuration, scripts, certificates, and persistent data for each service.</p><p>To create a directory structure</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="da2b7384-9649-454b-9f55-66ba7a61c4c6"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a9cf324b-df13-4773-948d-d1219bbc65f0">Use the following command to create a folder structure in your installation path:</ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="686e9dff-a2ce-410c-ab16-1f4c97da7ad5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[mkdir -p twc/{cassandra,data/{cassandra,certs,elasticsearch,twcloud,webapp,messaging},messaging,twcloud/{docker.scripts,twcloud},webapp/{docker.scripts,WebAppPlatform},zookeeper,elasticsearch}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="b65f0bc5-6ad7-4ece-b53b-80843a792eff">Verify using the following command:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d677d74-6123-48c1-8a27-acea0ff384b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[tree -d -L 2 twc]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="250"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image-2026-6-1_12-14-15.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Each service is built locally using its own Dockerfile.</p><h3>Preparing Containers</h3><p>The Docker network allows the containers to communicate with each other using container hostnames.</p><p>To create containers network</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cb18d8d8-ee48-418a-9f1c-5a5748dcb4c3">Create and verify a network for containers using the following commands:</ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b34ad020-39a1-4d21-9be7-b50df2adb24c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker network create twc-net
````html
docker network ls -f name=twc-net]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Generate certificates</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dc7ceec6-65d7-4698-b83b-f176bdd3dfcf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The example in this document uses a basic certificate configuration for demonstration purposes. For production SSL configuration, certificate replacement procedures, keystore management, and advanced security settings, refer to the “<a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MCS/2026x/teamwork-cloud-and-services/managing-ssl-certificate-247064513.html">Managing SSL certificate</a>” documentation.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The following example script generates certificates for the application.</p><ol><li data-uuid="d80cf259-ca2e-4062-a763-9c9ddd382ac0">Change to the twc directory.<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ce88c3c-0188-4ef6-8dd8-25d8be23a7f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd twc/]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="30bc64a6-0e1c-43dd-902c-f2e7e7828ee2">Create the script file <code>generateCerts.sh</code> and add the following content:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8760c6f5-cdf8-4870-8fd9-18cb7ad77830"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#!/usr/bin/env bash

#
# generateCerts.sh
#
# Generates a self-signed PKCS12 keystore and exported certificate
# for the provided Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN).
#
# Usage:
#   ./generateCerts.sh twcloud.example.com
#
# Example:
#   ./generateCerts.sh twcloud.company.internal
#

generate() {
  local fqdn=$1

  echo "Generating certificate for: $fqdn"

  keytool -genkeypair -keyalg RSA -sigalg SHA256withRSA -keysize 2048 -validity 3650 -keystore "${fqdn}.p12" -storetype pkcs12 -storepass nomagic -keypass nomagic -alias "$fqdn" -dname "CN=$fqdn" -ext "BasicConstraints:critical=ca:false" -ext "SAN=dns:$fqdn,dns:localhost,dns:host.docker.internal"

  keytool -export -keystore "${fqdn}.p12" -storepass nomagic -alias "$fqdn" -file "${fqdn}.crt"
}

fqdn=$1

if [[ -z "$fqdn" ]]; then
  local_hostname=$(hostname -f)

  echo "WARNING: No FQDN provided."
  read -rp "Use local hostname '$local_hostname'? [y/N]: " answer

  if [[ "$answer" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
    fqdn="$local_hostname"
  else
    echo "Please re-run the script with an explicit FQDN."
    exit 1
  fi
fi

generate "$fqdn"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="1b908ed2-9430-4050-957c-b30f5847f3d6">Make the script executable<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="56b365b5-4fff-4c2c-a03c-e4d7b388b7bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[chmod +x generateCerts.sh]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="db5d0cef-9dc8-4ee9-b309-11b2ec864c09">Run the script with your server FQDN:<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="91d9a07d-c40e-45a5-8d66-7d6ee510c9ad"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[./generateCerts.sh <fqdn>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="ad9d6e99-b97e-478a-b902-8650a95b03ad">Move the generated certificates to <code>data/certs/ directory.</code><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2984796d-ce1b-465e-89d3-c2678aaf11a0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[mv *.p12 *.crt data/certs/]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="282837a7-5c13-4309-ab9d-acbb83f00f8d"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f2522b1d-9d83-4648-8bc1-c117bcaffa96">Validate the generated certificates in <em>data/certs/ directory:</em></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a6cf619-8951-42ad-a3fb-fd9435f848fd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ls -l data/certs
keytool -list -v -keystore data/certs/<fqdn>.p12 -storepass nomagic]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><strong>Please follow the links below for container preparation.</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="14cf2cb2-da32-4d2f-aae1-78baf3077e61" /></p><p><br /></p><h3>Helpful command</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To follow the output (tail) of a specific container:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34c91068-7291-4400-a46f-961c7fda905a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker compose logs -f <Container Name or ID>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=309365855 space=MCS version=13 -->
## PAGE 00786: Preparing Elasticsearch container (optional)

- page_id: `309365855`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/309365855/Preparing+Elasticsearch+container+optional
- version_number: 13
- version_date: `2026-06-01T21:41:34.734+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Enterprise deployment > Preparing Docker Containers
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

These procedures are intended for users with Docker knowledge.We**do****not provide Docker support**, just provide guidelines on how to use it with our product. For more information about Docker, see[https://docs.docker.com/](https://docs.docker.com/)

##### Preparing Elasticsearch container (optional)

To build Elasticsearch service

1. Change to the twc directory and create the following file: *elasticsearch/docker-compose.yml* shell
2. Change permission for persistent storage shelltrue No custom Docker image build is required. This service use official Docker Hub images and are deployed manually using Docker Compose
3. Start the services by running the following commands: shelltrue
4. Validate elasticsearch shelltrue

**Stopping Service:**

To stop the running service, navigate to the service directory and run

```shell

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f69ef9ae-c5c2-432b-908e-4bb7161f04c9"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">These procedures are intended for users with Docker knowledge.<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">We<strong><span> </span>do </strong></span><strong style="text-align: left;">not provide Docker support</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">, just provide guidelines on how to use it with our product. For more information about Docker, see<span> </span></span><a style="text-align: left;" href="https://docs.docker.com/" class="">https://docs.docker.com/</a></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 style="text-align: left;">Preparing Elasticsearch  container (optional)</h3><p>To build Elasticsearch  service</p><p><br /></p><ol><li data-uuid="d971229d-ab7b-496e-84ab-482fcaed0364"><p>Change to the twc directory and create the following file:</p><p><em>elasticsearch/docker-compose.yml</em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8e2138a-cfa8-4c1e-bff1-e1e5b3968f3f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[services:
  elasticsearch:
    image: elasticsearch:8.19.0
    container_name: elasticsearch
    mem_limit: 4g
    ports:
      - "9200:9200"
    environment:
      - xpack.security.enabled=false
      - xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled=false
      - xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled=false
      - discovery.type=single-node
      - cluster.routing.allocation.disk.threshold_enabled=false
      - "ES_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms2g -Xmx2g"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "nc", "-z", "-w2", "localhost", "9200"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 60
    volumes:
      - ../data/elasticsearch:/usr/share/elasticsearch/data
    networks:
      - twc-net

networks:
  twc-net:
    external: true]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="5856ba30-0b4a-4f53-98e1-28f86d3b6849">Change permission for persistent storage<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b48be3c1-aa6b-4685-b604-475ffa28c017"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[chown -R 1000:1000 data/elasticsearch]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48967d38-a6df-4531-85fb-59302873b17d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>No custom Docker image build is required. This service use official Docker Hub images and are deployed manually using Docker Compose</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="4e34a973-7516-40e3-a724-d70108190fc2">Start the services by running the following commands:<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e4d4d38b-661b-4952-83eb-755aa5460a1a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd elasticsearch
docker compose up -d]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="8e2001a9-fbaf-443f-9378-b65a8176bc00">Validate elasticsearch<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4ebe9ace-5c65-42b9-88b9-a0e37d8ca911"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker exec -it elasticsearch nc -zv localhost 9200]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><br /></li></ol><p><strong>Stopping Service:</strong></p><p> To stop the running service, navigate to the service directory and run </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="caa9a788-439f-430d-86bb-e7c8605b5e38"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd twc/elasticsearch
docker compose down]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /> </p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=291833012 space=MCS version=63 -->
## PAGE 00787: Preparing Teamwork Cloud container

- page_id: `291833012`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/291833012/Preparing+Teamwork+Cloud+container
- version_number: 63
- version_date: `2026-06-02T23:26:25.786+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Enterprise deployment > Preparing Docker Containers
- labels: ['twc', 'mcs']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

do

not provide Docker support

https://docs.docker.com/

Teamwork Cloud is the core backend platform for the deployment.

##### Downloading Teamwork Cloud software

To download the Teamwork Cloud software

1. Download the <*product_name>_<version_number>_linux64* .zip file from [https://software.3ds.com](https://software.3ds.com). See 3DS Media list .
2. Copy < product_name>_<version_number>_linux64 .zip to the twc directory on the server, then change directory to the twc directory: shelltrue
3. Unzip < product_name>_<version_number>_linux64 .zip and copy all the files from TeamworkCloud directory using below commands. This step copies Teamwork Cloud binaries into image build context. shelltrue__linux64.zip
cp -r CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/. twcloud/twcloud/]]>

##### Preparing Teamwork Cloud image

Please check the correct component version in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Third-Party Component Compatibility' space=''] page and edit the Java versions in the configuration files.

1. Create the following files:
  1. twcloud/Dockerfile text
  2. twcloud/docker-compose.yml Change "FQDN" to your hostname. Elasticsearch is OPTIONAL and should only be enabled if Elasticsearch service is deployed and reachable. shell-
 -Desi.httpd.ssl.keystorePath=certs/.p12
 -Dakka.cluster.seed-nodes.0=akka://twcloud@twcloud:2552
 -Desi.persistence.datastax-java-driver.basic.contact-points.0=cassandra:9042
 -Desi.auth.url=https://:8443
 -Desi.zookeeper.url=zookeeper:2181
 -Desi.indexer.enabled=false
 -Desi.query.es.node.host=elasticsearch
 -Desi.server.actor.query.component-enabled=false
 -Desi.query.es.node.port=9200
 volumes:
 - ../data/certs:/twcloud/certs:ro
 networks:
 - twc-net
 healthcheck:
 test: ["CMD", "nc", "-z", "-w2", "localhost", "3579"]
 interval: 10s
 timeout: 5s
 retries: 30

networks:
 twc-net:
 external: true]]>
  3. *twcloud/docker.scripts/entrypoint.sh **(make it executable)*** shell
  4. Make the script executable shelltrue
  5. *twcloud/docker.scripts/init.sh **(make it executable)*** shell
  6. Make the script executable shelltrue
  7. Build the image by running the following commands: shelltrue

2. Start Teamwork Cloud by running the following command:

```shell

```

```text

```

3. Run the following health check command to verify that TWC is operational:

```shell

```

4. Use the following URLs:

- To connect from a modeling tool client (with default port 3579): shell]]>
- To access REST API/Swagger Page: shell:8111]]> You can apply the Teamwork Cloud license[via the web UI](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/TWCloudTWRT/pages/127967448/Applying+Teamwork+Cloud+license)or[REST API](https://osmc.nomagic.com/).

**Stopping Service:**

1. To stop the running service, navigate to the service directory and run: shelltrue

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c5275f1-b079-4131-ae08-dd046af2e6cb"><ac:rich-text-body>These procedures are intended for users with Docker knowledge. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">We <strong>do </strong></span><strong>not provide Docker support</strong>, just provide guidelines on how to use it with our product. For more information about Docker, see <a href="https://docs.docker.com/" rel="nofollow">https://docs.docker.com/</a></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Teamwork Cloud is the core backend platform for the deployment.</p><h3>Downloading Teamwork Cloud software</h3><p>To download the Teamwork Cloud software</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="9cdbbcea-215a-4b76-9f9c-6a20099ee5ac"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="56387ac3-35a4-44c9-8b83-c5ec2637baa3">Download the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">&lt;</span><em style="text-align: left;">product_name&gt;_&lt;version_number&gt;_linux64</em></ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="56387ac3-35a4-44c9-8b83-c5ec2637baa3">.zip</ac:inline-comment-marker> </em>file from <span><a href="https://software.3ds.com">https://software.3ds.com</a>.</span> See <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/2026x/3ds-media-list-for-2026x-release-277479887.html">3DS Media list</a>.</li><li data-uuid="999d0cc2-bdd9-4005-9026-409a0c312700">Copy <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">&lt;</span><em style="text-align: left;">product_name&gt;_&lt;version_number&gt;_linux64</em><em>.zip</em> to the <em>twc directory </em>on the server, then change directory to the <em>twc directory:</em><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="928398ae-ea5e-4e49-8e04-59bdf3025c18"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd twc/]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="492c5c0c-9453-4221-860b-df6f0f26a224">Unzip <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">&lt;</span><em style="text-align: left;">product_name&gt;_&lt;version_number&gt;_linux64</em><em>.zip </em>and copy all the files from TeamworkCloud directory using below commands. This step copies Teamwork Cloud binaries into image build context.<em><br /></em><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce56bbb2-3ac9-4ce0-bcda-01b45c7848a8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[unzip <product_name>_<version_number>_linux64.zip
cp -r CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/. twcloud/twcloud/]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Preparing Teamwork Cloud image</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c731b43c-b045-4ac7-9eeb-c57bc36ba4c2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Please check the correct component version in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Third-Party Component Compatibility" /></ac:link> page and edit the Java versions in the configuration files.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li data-uuid="a1840aab-b989-4c84-8c87-28b70c05b24d">Create the following files: <ol><li data-uuid="a24ac897-44ef-4eb1-bbdb-ce64ad979d4d"><em>twcloud/Dockerfile</em><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d419826b-eec2-4fda-80b1-e71b5a76ccff"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jdk
RUN useradd -m twcloud
COPY twcloud /twcloud
COPY docker.scripts /scripts
RUN chmod +x /scripts/*.sh
RUN sh /scripts/init.sh
RUN chown -R twcloud:twcloud /twcloud /scripts
WORKDIR /twcloud
EXPOSE 8111 3579 10002
VOLUME /certs
USER twcloud
CMD ["/scripts/entrypoint.sh"]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="6be3ee67-b9b4-49d7-bcd4-e2c1749792d2"><em>twcloud/docker-compose.yml</em><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="261416cf-5166-4ce6-89fd-f629f75ecd3f"><ac:rich-text-body>Change &quot;FQDN&quot; to your hostname.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6cd6b97c-6865-4ca1-a797-3f931575b578"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Elasticsearch is OPTIONAL and should only be enabled if Elasticsearch service is deployed and reachable.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="25e76ba4-0a9f-43af-b6f5-0fca6482ffb0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[services:
  twcloud:
    build: .
    image: localhost/twc_twcloud:latest
    container_name: twcloud
    mem_limit: 12g
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8111:8111"
      - "3579:3579"
      - "10002:10002"
    environment:
      TWCLOUD_OPTS: >-
        -Desi.httpd.ssl.keystorePath=certs/<FQDN>.p12
        -Dakka.cluster.seed-nodes.0=akka://twcloud@twcloud:2552
        -Desi.persistence.datastax-java-driver.basic.contact-points.0=cassandra:9042
        -Desi.auth.url=https://<FQDN>:8443
        -Desi.zookeeper.url=zookeeper:2181
        -Desi.indexer.enabled=false
        -Desi.query.es.node.host=elasticsearch
        -Desi.server.actor.query.component-enabled=false
        -Desi.query.es.node.port=9200
    volumes:
      - ../data/certs:/twcloud/certs:ro
    networks:
      - twc-net
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "nc", "-z", "-w2", "localhost", "3579"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 30

networks:
  twc-net:
    external: true]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="594a9ab5-5776-4d94-9c4a-f59313026389"><p><em>twcloud/docker.scripts/entrypoint.sh  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);"><strong>(make it executable)</strong></span></em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e3e9d708-b7ee-4e48-963b-7ec3b6db2dbd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#!/bin/bash

echo "JAVA_HOME=${JAVA_HOME}"
echo "TWCLOUD_OPTS=${TWCLOUD_OPTS}"

echo ======================================================
free -h
echo ======================================================
java -version
echo ======================================================

ls -la

sed -i 's/\r//g' jvm.options
sed -i 's/\r//g' configuration/application.conf
echo ======================================================
cat configuration/application.conf
echo ======================================================
cat jvm.options
echo ======================================================
echo "Starting..."
./twcloud
echo "Exiting... $?"
echo ======================================================]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="53e76146-1664-41aa-b059-8d306fe96e4f">Make the script executable<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2fd9e4d6-7121-411e-af09-1125e90d22b7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[chmod +x twcloud/docker.scripts/entrypoint.sh]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="e598188a-5738-4950-a94d-25e0fd1a1495"><p><em>twcloud/docker.scripts/init.sh <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);"><strong>(make it executable)</strong></span></em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5bbc7a2-b315-4ec0-8eb4-3659598f5739"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#!/bin/bash
echo "=== INIT ==="

apt-get -y update && apt-get -y install apt-utils sudo wget netcat-openbsd && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

echo "/=== INIT ===/"
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="1b79c725-e9d3-4e72-94e1-826be413ef30">Make the script executable<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a95080da-467b-40a6-a559-8561403606bd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[chmod +x twcloud/docker.scripts/init.sh]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="63a07702-df54-4a70-b143-ca68997eafe0"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0cc1c262-c3eb-47f5-ac16-4b0368a26076">Build the image by running the following commands:</ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c43aaab-b2e0-4acf-95ad-75daec8f8f85"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd twcloud
docker compose build --no-cache]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li></ol><p> 2. Start Teamwork Cloud by running the following command:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5a9c025-a59e-4777-bb6a-2e00c24587fb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker compose up -d]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><pre><br /></pre><p> 3. Run the following health check command to verify that TWC is operational:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d62c382-dcd6-40cf-9fbc-343db6698bb9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker exec -it twcloud nc -vz localhost 3579]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> 4. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Use the following URLs:</span></p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="ecfe0fc4-626a-44e8-87ef-57c0aa6fd5db"><p><span>To connect from a modeling tool client (with default port 3579):</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="26c52648-9fe7-43d9-80d9-2c7422f1c89e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<FQDN>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="66724b12-f201-4fcb-8d40-dd5f3cbe65c2"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> To access REST API/Swagger Page:</span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7539bb55-acf2-439f-9572-50538b456439"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[https://<FQDN>:8111]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4a0dca19-93a3-41bc-aea4-a43da083c79e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">You can apply the Teamwork Cloud license<span> </span></span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/TWCloudTWRT/pages/127967448/Applying+Teamwork+Cloud+license">via the web UI</a><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>or<span> </span></span><a class="external-link" style="text-align: left;" href="https://osmc.nomagic.com/">REST API</a><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><p><strong>Stopping Service:</strong></p><ol><li data-uuid="a9c39c97-7107-4fd4-a05a-c7da03a5914b">To stop the running service, navigate to the service directory and run: <ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="91b9bb25-d655-4142-a4e3-2da1480ed649"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd twc/twcloud
docker compose down]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046460 space=MCS version=4 -->
## PAGE 00788: Preparing the operating system

- page_id: `247046460`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046460/Preparing+the+operating+system
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-10-27T10:57:29.802+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Installation > Installation on Linux
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Prior to installing Cassandra, it is important to understand how Cassandra utilizes disk space. Disk space depends on usage. The database writes data to disk when appending data to the commit log for durability and when flushing memtables to SSTable data files for persistent storage. The commit log has a different access pattern (read/writes ratio) than the pattern for accessing data from SSTables. This is more important for spinning disks than for SSDs.

SSTables are periodically compacted. Compaction improves performance by merging and rewriting data as well as discarding old data. However, depending on the type and size of the compactions, disk utilization and data directory volume temporarily increase during compaction. For this reason, be sure to leave an adequate amount of free disk space available on a node.

Cassandra's data and commit logs should not under any circumstances be placed on the drive where the operating system is installed. Ideally, a server should have 3-4 drives or partitions. The root, /, or OS partition can be used as the target for the application. The /data partition should have adequate amounts of storage to accommodate your data. The /logs partition should hold your commit logs and (unless it is SSD) be on a different physical disk than the /data partition. The /backup partition should be allocated for backups.

For information about selecting hardware, see [https://cassandra.apache.org/doc/latest/cassandra/managing/operating/hardware.html](https://cassandra.apache.org/doc/latest/cassandra/managing/operating/hardware.html)

To achieve adequate performance, create separate partitions, ideally on separate drives. This helps to avoid I/O contention. We recommend 3 separate storage devicesor at least 3 separate partitions for non-production environments:

1. */opt/local*(the first block device should contain the operating system as well as a mount for the programs) .
2. The second block device ( NVMe/SSD ) should contain a mount point at /data (the device must have high storage capacity for all the data).
3. The third block device ( NVMe/SSD, but does not need to be of high capacity) should contain a mount point at /logs.

If you use SSD, the device can be a partition on the same block device as the /data partition. All partitions should be formatted using the XFS file system, and there should not be a swap partition. The /backup partition can be a mount on a shared storage device, but should not be on the same physical drive as the /data partition. 
 
See an example of the contents of*/etc/fstab* after partitioning, where the partitions were created using LVM (without a mount for the /backup partition).

```text

```

In the above example:

- Disk 1 contains the following partitions: /opt/local (40GB) and / (rest of the drive capacity).
- Disk 2 (the disk with the highest capacity) contains the /data partition (at least 250GB). Due to the way compactions are handled by Cassandra, up to 50% of headroom may be needed in a worst-case scenario.
- Disk 3 contains the /logs partition (at least 10 GB).
- You should also create an additional mount for backups. Unlike the data and commit log partitions, which should be on SSD storage, this mount can be of any type (including centralized storage such as a SAN or NAS). It should have at least the same capacity as the /data partition.

The partitioning scheme above is an example. Internal security protocols in your organization may dictate that other directories may not be located in the main partition. During the installation, all applications will be installed in*/opt/local*. By default, Cassandra will be installed in */var/lib*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Prior to installing Cassandra, it is important to understand how Cassandra utilizes disk space. Disk space depends on usage. The database writes data to disk when appending data to the commit log for durability and when flushing memtables to SSTable data files for persistent storage. The commit log has a different access pattern (read/writes ratio) than the pattern for accessing data from SSTables. This is more important for spinning disks than for SSDs.</p><p class="p">SSTables are periodically compacted. Compaction improves performance by merging and rewriting data as well as discarding old data. However, depending on the type and size of the compactions, disk utilization and data directory volume temporarily increase during compaction. For this reason, be sure to leave an adequate amount of free disk space available on a node.</p><p>Cassandra's data and commit logs should not under any circumstances be placed on the drive where the operating system is installed. Ideally, a server should have 3-4 drives or partitions. The root, /, or OS partition can be used as the target for the application. The /data partition should have adequate amounts of storage to accommodate your data. The /logs partition should hold your commit logs and (unless it is SSD) be on a different physical disk than the /data partition. The /backup partition should be allocated for backups.</p><p>For information about selecting hardware, see <a href="https://cassandra.apache.org/doc/latest/cassandra/managing/operating/hardware.html" class="">https://cassandra.apache.org/doc/latest/cassandra/managing/operating/hardware.html</a></p><p>To achieve adequate performance, create separate partitions, ideally on separate drives. This helps to avoid I/O contention. We recommend 3 separate <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">storage devices </span>or at least 3 separate partitions for non-production environments:</p><ol><li data-uuid="c7cbe08a-90e3-4d70-876b-e9dbed3444b9"><span><em>/opt/local </em>(the first block device should contain the operating system as well as a mount for the programs)</span>.</li><li data-uuid="514d2517-f0b2-4a4a-84dd-71739ddb53b9">The second block device (<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">NVMe/SSD</span>) should contain a mount point at /data (the device must have high storage capacity for all the data).</li><li data-uuid="9d07fbee-62f5-4fd6-8df0-ee50852a86d4">The third block device (<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">NVMe/SSD,</span> but does not need to be of high capacity) should contain a mount point at /logs.</li></ol><p>If you use SSD, the device can be a partition on the same block device as the /data partition. All partitions should be formatted using the XFS file system, and there should not be a swap partition. The /backup partition can be a mount on a shared storage device, but should not be on the same physical drive as the /data partition.<br /><br />See an example of the contents of<em> /etc/fstab</em> after partitioning, where the partitions were created using LVM (without a mount for the /backup partition).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6569206b-ae3b-4996-986d-483d1008619b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">fstab</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#
# /etc/fstab
# Created by anaconda on Tue May  2 16:31:05 2017
#
# Accessible filesystems, by reference, are maintained under '/dev/disk'
# See man pages fstab(5), findfs(8), mount(8) and/or blkid(8) for more info
#
/dev/mapper/cl_twccentos7-root /                       xfs     defaults        0 0
/dev/mapper/cl_twccentos7-data /data                   xfs     defaults        0 0
/dev/mapper/cl_twccentos7-logs /logs                   xfs     defaults        0 0
/dev/mapper/cl_twccentos7-opt_local /opt/local         xfs     defaults        0 0]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In the above example:</p><ul><li data-uuid="c14a9b06-f426-4752-ac03-7ae0f67d0b95">Disk 1 contains the following partitions:  <em>/opt/local</em> (40GB) and<em> /</em> (rest of the drive capacity).</li><li data-uuid="0dd5707f-f311-45a4-ac3f-e65c221d60d8">Disk 2 (the disk with the highest capacity) contains the <em>/data</em> partition (at least 250GB). Due to the way compactions are handled by Cassandra, up to 50% of headroom may be needed in a worst-case scenario.</li><li data-uuid="b26f643d-8120-467b-970a-5025e1108408">Disk 3 contains the<em> /logs</em> partition (at least 10 GB).</li><li data-uuid="054e9849-641c-45c6-bca1-c5c654692b10">You should also create an additional mount for backups. Unlike the data and commit log partitions, which should be on SSD storage, this mount can be of any type (including centralized storage such as a SAN or NAS). It should have at least the same capacity as the /data partition.</li></ul><p>The partitioning scheme above is an example. Internal security protocols in your organization may dictate that other directories may not be located in the main partition. During the installation, all applications will be installed in<em> /opt/local</em>. By default, Cassandra will be installed in <em>/var/lib</em>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=291833023 space=MCS version=39 -->
## PAGE 00789: Preparing Web Application Platform documentation container

- page_id: `291833023`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/291833023/Preparing+Web+Application+Platform+documentation+container
- version_number: 39
- version_date: `2026-06-08T19:48:58.953+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Enterprise deployment > Preparing Docker Containers
- labels: ['twc', 'mcs']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

do

not provide Docker support

https://docs.docker.com/

Web Application Platform provides a user-facing UI and an authentication entry point.

**Prerequisites**

To build the Docker image, ensure the following inputs are available in the build context:

- Web Application Platform package directory
  - WebAppPlatform/ (contains application binaries and configuration files)
- Web application archive(s)
  - <service_name>.war files placed under: WebAppPlatform/webapps/

Note: The WebAppPlatform package already includes required configuration files such as `logback.xml` and `webappplatform.properties`

Note that running multiple Docker images without resource limitation canconsume too much of the host machine’s memory and cause "out of memory" kills. For more information on how to set resource constraints for a Docker container, refer to[https://docs.docker.com/config/containers/resource_constraints/](https://docs.docker.com/config/containers/resource_constraints/).

##### Downloading Web Application Platform software

To download the Web Application Platform software

1. Download the Web_Application_Platform_<version>_war_files.zip file from https://software.3ds.com . See 3DS Media list .
2. Copy Web_Application_Platform_<version>_files.zip to twc on the server, extract it. and copy all the files from WebAppPlatform using below commands. shelltrue Unzip Web_Application_Platform_<version>_war_files.zip, and copy all or required war files to webapps folder shelltrue

##### Building Web Application Platform image

Please check the correct component version in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Third-Party Component Compatibility' space=''] page and edit the Java version in the configuration files.

1. Create the following files:
  1. *webapp/Dockerfile* shell
  2. *webapp/docker-compose.yml* Change AUTH_SERVER_IP, KEYSTORE_FILE & KEY_ALIAS values as per your environment shell-
 -Xms8g -Xmx16g -Dfile.encoding=UTF-8
 ZOOKEEPER_HOST: zookeeper
 CASSANDRA_HOST: cassandra
 TWC_HOST: twcloud
 AUTH_SERVER_IP: MESSAGING_EXTERNAL: "true"
 MESSAGING_HOST: messaging
 MESSAGING_PORT: "61616"
 MESSAGING_SECURITY_ENABLED: "true"
 MESSAGING_SECURITY_USERNAME: artemis
 MESSAGING_SECURITY_PASSWORD: artemis
 REDIRECT_WHITELIST: https://
 KEYSTORE_FILE: certs/.p12
 KEY_ALIAS: ES_ENABLED: "false"
 volumes:
 - ../data/certs:/usr/local/Certs:ro
 - ../data/certs:/usr/local/tomcat/certs:ro
 - ../data/webapp/logs:/usr/local/tomcat/logs
 networks:
 - twc-net
 healthcheck:
 test: ["CMD", "nc", "-z", "localhost", "8443"]
 interval: 15s
 timeout: 5s
 retries: 30

networks:
 twc-net:
 external: true]]>
  3. Change permissions for host logs directory shelltrue
  4. *webapp/docker.scripts/run.sh(make it executable)* shell&1 | tee -a logs/output.log
]]>
  5. Make the script executable true
2. Build the image by running the following command: shelltrue
3. Start Teamwork Cloud by running the following command: shelltrue
4. Run a hea lth check by executing the following command to check if Cassandra is operational: shelltrue
5. Verify status: shelltrue All services should be in **Up (healthy)** state:.

**Stopping Service:**

1. To stop the running service, navigate to the service directory and run:

```shell

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c5275f1-b079-4131-ae08-dd046af2e6cb"><ac:rich-text-body>These procedures are intended for users with Docker knowledge. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">We <strong>do </strong></span><strong>not provide Docker support</strong>, just provide guidelines on how to use it with our product. For more information about Docker, see <a href="https://docs.docker.com/" rel="nofollow">https://docs.docker.com/</a></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Web <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1496a550-e0c2-4b0d-bc35-7600e1c81763">Application Plat</ac:inline-comment-marker>form provides a user-facing UI and an authentication entry point.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="127f8adc-e9fe-4cbe-9ec0-2d5b7f318ec7"><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: left;"><strong>Prerequisites</strong></p><p style="text-align: left;">To build the Docker image, ensure the following inputs are available in the build context:</p><ul><li data-uuid="da6c3f37-e7b9-4c90-acc2-05c18d619176"><strong>Web Application Platform package directory</strong><ul><li><code>WebAppPlatform/</code> (contains application binaries and configuration files)</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="85fa9121-32f8-4e12-869b-d34b4a546176"><strong>Web application archive(s)</strong><ul><li><code>&lt;service_name&gt;.war</code> files placed under: WebAppPlatform/webapps/</li></ul></li></ul><p>Note: The WebAppPlatform package already includes required configuration files such as <code>logback.xml</code> and <code>webappplatform.properties</code></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e51e2a74-6d60-4caa-9b7e-074d3b7c4bb9"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Note that running multiple Docker images without resource limitation can<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">consume too much of the host machine’s memory and cause &quot;out of memory&quot; kills. For more information on how to set resource constraints for a Docker container, refer to<span> </span><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.docker.com/config/containers/resource_constraints/">https://docs.docker.com/config/containers/resource_constraints/</a>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Downloading Web Application Platform software</h3><p>To download the Web Application Platform software</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="3a08a0c0-b028-4893-904b-5131794e0389"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="62c70374-3934-4ee2-8e48-6cc3efecbce3">Download the </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="62c70374-3934-4ee2-8e48-6cc3efecbce3">Web_Application_Platform_</ac:inline-comment-marker><span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="62c70374-3934-4ee2-8e48-6cc3efecbce3">&lt;version&gt;</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="62c70374-3934-4ee2-8e48-6cc3efecbce3">_war_files.zip</ac:inline-comment-marker></em> file from <a href="https://software.3ds.com">https://software.3ds.com</a>. See <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/2026x/3ds-media-list-for-2026x-release-277479887.html">3DS Media list</a>.</li><li data-uuid="e66365c0-8d41-4bb1-bbba-e63889e47bbd">Copy <em>Web_Application_Platform_<span>&lt;version&gt;</span>_files.zip</em> to <em>twc</em> on the server, extract it. and copy all the files from WebAppPlatform using below commands.<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="03145e9f-8fca-4294-a176-014d03e0747f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp -r CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform   webapp/WebAppPlatform
cp -r CATIANoMagicServices/Prince webapp]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro>Unzip <em>Web_Application_Platform_<span>&lt;version&gt;</span>_war_files.zip, and copy all or required war files  to webapps folder</em><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="42c30f9f-1d5c-4897-b6ff-359e008a6e35"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp -r *.war webapp/WebAppPlatform/webapps]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Building <span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">Web Application Platform</span> image  </h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c731b43c-b045-4ac7-9eeb-c57bc36ba4c2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Please check the correct component version in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Third-Party Component Compatibility" /></ac:link> page and edit the Java version in the configuration files.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li data-uuid="557171fc-3655-4aca-a96c-d88088ea75d8">Create the following files:<ol><li><p><em>webapp/Dockerfile</em></p><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e106403-a6f5-4f8a-af1c-53b417886255"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[FROM tomcat:10.1-jre21-temurin-jammy

ARG USER_UID=5000
ARG USER_GID=5000

# Fonts are required ONLY for document-exporter service (PDF/document rendering)
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y wget netcat-openbsd net-tools dialog apt-utils ttf-mscorefonts-installer && \
    echo "ttf-mscorefonts-installer msttcorefonts/accepted-mscorefonts-eula select true" | debconf-set-selections && \
    fc-cache -fv && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN mkdir -p /Certs /Prince /scripts /usr/local/tomcat

RUN rm -rf /usr/local/tomcat/webapps/* /usr/local/tomcat/webapps.dist

COPY docker.scripts /scripts
COPY WebAppPlatform/ /usr/local/tomcat/
COPY Prince /Prince

RUN chmod +x /scripts/run.sh

RUN groupadd --gid ${USER_GID} tomcat && \
    useradd --uid ${USER_UID} --gid ${USER_GID} --home-dir /usr/local/tomcat --shell /usr/sbin/nologin --comment "Tomcat User" tomcat && \
    chown -R tomcat:tomcat /usr/local/tomcat /scripts /Prince /Certs

USER tomcat

EXPOSE 8443

CMD ["/scripts/run.sh"]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p><em>webapp/<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9e08593c-e216-4cdc-b1b6-c6c1512375d8">docker</ac:inline-comment-marker>-compose.yml</em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd128c8c-f322-4fd8-b8d3-c839ce5f6804"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Change AUTH_SERVER_IP, KEYSTORE_FILE &amp; KEY_ALIAS values as per your environment</p><pre><br /></pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b502c899-97e4-42e9-847d-36947ed91360"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[services:
  webapp:
    image: localhost/twc_webapp:latest
    container_name: webapp
    build:
      context: .
    ports:
      - "8443:8443"
    environment:
      JAVA_OPTS: >-
        -Xms8g -Xmx16g -Dfile.encoding=UTF-8
      ZOOKEEPER_HOST: zookeeper
      CASSANDRA_HOST: cassandra
      TWC_HOST: twcloud
      AUTH_SERVER_IP: <AUTH_SERVER>
      MESSAGING_EXTERNAL: "true"
      MESSAGING_HOST: messaging
      MESSAGING_PORT: "61616"
      MESSAGING_SECURITY_ENABLED: "true"
      MESSAGING_SECURITY_USERNAME: artemis
      MESSAGING_SECURITY_PASSWORD: artemis
      REDIRECT_WHITELIST: https://
      KEYSTORE_FILE: certs/<keystore>.p12
      KEY_ALIAS: <keystore>
      ES_ENABLED: "false"
    volumes:
      - ../data/certs:/usr/local/Certs:ro
      - ../data/certs:/usr/local/tomcat/certs:ro
      - ../data/webapp/logs:/usr/local/tomcat/logs
    networks:
      - twc-net
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "nc", "-z", "localhost", "8443"]
      interval: 15s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 30

networks:
  twc-net:
    external: true]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="4ded020f-2bf4-4f0b-8349-50a60a2e39fa">Change permissions for host logs directory <ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aa8d240e-652f-4eed-96c0-04d4f5e42f36"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[chown -R 5000:5000 data/webapp/logs
chmod -R 775 data/webapp/logs]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="59bc3e27-ca02-489d-ae54-ffae3c8912c8"><p><em>webapp/docker.scripts/run.sh<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);"> (make it executable)</span></em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3a86f8ef-74b5-49eb-896b-15a5da1af8f5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#!/bin/bash

echo "ZOOKEEPER_HOST=${ZOOKEEPER_HOST}"
echo "CASSANDRA_HOST=${CASSANDRA_HOST}"
echo "TWC_HOST=${TWC_HOST}"
echo "AUTH_SERVER_IP=${AUTH_SERVER_IP}"
echo "MESSAGING_EXTERNAL=${MESSAGING_EXTERNAL}"
echo "MESSAGING_HOST=${MESSAGING_HOST}"
echo "MESSAGING_PORT=${MESSAGING_PORT}"

if [ -n "${REDIRECT_WHITELIST}" ]; then
  sed -i \
    "s|^authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist=.*$|authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist=${REDIRECT_WHITELIST}|" \
    shared/conf/authserver.properties
fi

if [ -n "${CASSANDRA_HOST}" ]; then
  sed -i \
    "s|^cassandra.contactPoints=.*$|cassandra.contactPoints=${CASSANDRA_HOST}|" \
    shared/conf/authserver.properties
fi

sed -i \
  "s|^#authentication.client.ids=.*$|authentication.client.ids=MAGICDRAW,webApplicationPlatform,twcSynchronizationManager,twc-rest-api|" \
  shared/conf/authserver.properties

if [ -n "${TWC_HOST}" ]; then
  sed -i \
    "s|^twc.ip=.*$|twc.ip=${TWC_HOST}|" \
    shared/conf/webappplatform.properties
fi

if [ -n "${ZOOKEEPER_HOST}" ]; then
  sed -i \
    "s|^zookeeper.server.ip=.*$|zookeeper.server.ip=${ZOOKEEPER_HOST}|" \
    shared/conf/webappplatform.properties
fi

if [ "${MESSAGING_EXTERNAL}" = "true" ]; then
  sed -i \
    "s|^messaging.server.start=.*$|messaging.server.start=false|" \
    shared/conf/webappplatform.properties

  sed -i \
    "s|^messaging.server.ip=.*$|messaging.server.ip=${MESSAGING_HOST}|" \
    shared/conf/webappplatform.properties

  sed -i \
    "s|^messaging.server.port=.*$|messaging.server.port=${MESSAGING_PORT}|" \
    shared/conf/webappplatform.properties
fi

if [ "${MESSAGING_SECURITY_ENABLED}" = "true" ]; then
  sed -i \
    "s|.*messaging.server.security.enabled=.*$|messaging.server.security.enabled=true|" \
    shared/conf/webappplatform.properties

  sed -i \
    "s|.*messaging.user.name=.*$|messaging.user.name=${MESSAGING_SECURITY_USERNAME}|" \
    shared/conf/webappplatform.properties

  sed -i \
    "s|.*messaging.user.password=.*$|messaging.user.password=${MESSAGING_SECURITY_PASSWORD}|" \
    shared/conf/webappplatform.properties
fi

if [ -n "${AUTH_SERVER_IP}" ]; then
  sed -i \
    "s|^authentication.server.ip=.*$|authentication.server.ip=${AUTH_SERVER_IP}|" \
    shared/conf/webappplatform.properties
fi

if [ -n "${KEYSTORE_FILE}" ]; then
  sed -i \
    "s|^authentication.server.key-store=.*$|authentication.server.key-store=${KEYSTORE_FILE}|" \
    shared/conf/authserver.properties
fi

if [ -n "${KEY_ALIAS}" ]; then
  sed -i \
    "s|^authentication.server.key-alias=.*$|authentication.server.key-alias=${KEY_ALIAS}|" \
    shared/conf/authserver.properties
fi
exec bash ./bin/catalina.sh run 2>&1 | tee -a logs/output.log
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p></li><li data-uuid="dbf47860-08ec-4899-9080-cfa03c48e762">Make the script executable<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ef4df0f3-e818-4e3b-a9c5-f27b3c0b9d42"><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[chmod +x webapp/docker.scripts/run.sh]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="6532be77-bcbe-43d9-970a-5b0f3ec53aa6">Build the image by running the following command:<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="20c88b7d-46bf-483b-b773-6ed3291f2ffd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd webapp
docker compose build --no-cache webapp]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="4021dbab-f523-44b3-88d1-3682daebfef8"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Start Teamwork Cloud by running the following command:</span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb4e2809-0488-4b6e-bf98-40b2d77399cf"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker compose up -d]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="b8465f8e-2deb-4628-b17a-30d62e156088">Run a <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6cf1a182-a09e-4db2-989a-162ed165705c">hea</ac:inline-comment-marker>lth check by executing the following command to check if Cassandra is operational:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="060cb12b-bb37-4d9b-b2f5-84485fca1148"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker exec webapp nc -zv localhost 8443]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="354f825f-144d-4752-8733-6562705bc277">Verify status:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1f6764ff-4885-43c4-aa7f-9475bf6e81c8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker ps -a]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><p>All services should be in <strong>Up (healthy)</strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e6db9daf-a528-41ef-9b68-e014569f51e3">state</ac:inline-comment-marker>:.</p></li></ol><p><strong>Stopping Service:</strong></p><p>1. To stop the running service, navigate to the service directory and run:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb9e2bc4-e48f-4aec-bcd7-a80d921719d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd twc/webapp
docker compose down]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=291833016 space=MCS version=23 -->
## PAGE 00790: Preparing ZooKeeper  container

- page_id: `291833016`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/291833016/Preparing+ZooKeeper+container
- version_number: 23
- version_date: `2026-06-02T23:18:52.061+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Enterprise deployment > Preparing Docker Containers
- labels: ['twc', 'mcs']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

do

not provide Docker support

https://docs.docker.com/

Zookeeper provides coordination services for Teamwork Cloud components.

##### Preparing ZooKeeper container

Please check the correct component version in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Third-Party Component Compatibility' space=''] page and edit the ZooKeeper version in the configuration file.

To build ZooKeeper service

1. Change to the twc directory and create the following file: *zookeeper/docker-compose.yml* shell No custom Docker image build is required. This service use official Docker Hub images and are deployed manually using Docker Compose
2. Start the services by running the following commands: shelltrue
3. Validate zookeeper shelltrue

**Stopping Service:**

To stop the running service, navigate to the service directory and run

```shell

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c5275f1-b079-4131-ae08-dd046af2e6cb"><ac:rich-text-body>These procedures are intended for users with Docker knowledge. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">We<strong> do </strong></span><strong>not provide Docker support</strong>, just provide guidelines on how to use it with our product. For more information about Docker, see <a href="https://docs.docker.com/" rel="nofollow">https://docs.docker.com/</a></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Zookeeper provides coordination services for Teamwork Cloud components.</p><h3>Preparing ZooKeeper  container</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c731b43c-b045-4ac7-9eeb-c57bc36ba4c2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Please check the correct component version in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Third-Party Component Compatibility" /></ac:link> page and edit the ZooKeeper version in the configuration file.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To build ZooKeeper  service</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="fc49fcc4-7e25-48e9-8a39-be7177197521">Change to the twc directory and create the following file:<p><em>zookeeper/docker-<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="23155b8f-f921-4d2a-ba37-4d0d443ecd75">compose</ac:inline-comment-marker>.yml</em></p><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bbad16e3-f62b-4ccb-921c-5ae2b80247db"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[services:
  zookeeper:
    image: zookeeper:3.9.4
    container_name: zookeeper
    mem_limit: 2g
    environment:
      - ZOO_4LW_COMMANDS_WHITELIST=ruok
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "echo ruok | nc localhost 2181 | grep imok"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
    networks:
      - twc-net

networks:
  twc-net:
    external: true]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33f09aa8-fd76-49a6-948d-d8f6dc823590"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">No custom Docker image build is required. This service use official Docker Hub images and are deployed manually using Docker Compose</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="70f52ad9-aaa8-44a1-88cd-252e8d18f099">Start the services by running the following commands:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="af2b6a07-0091-4e69-bce6-871b906327bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd zookeeper
docker compose up -d ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="f82c708b-4738-4175-a7c7-ffb7df05f2a6">Validate zookeeper<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="155c5ce7-50c0-4fe4-99cf-7282337de385"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[# Validate ZooKeeper
docker exec -it zookeeper nc -zv localhost 2181]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><strong>Stopping Service:</strong></p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"> To stop the running service, navigate to the service directory and run </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2fcab72e-8e38-4034-9fd0-9b8ab8e17207"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd twc/zookeeper
docker compose down]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047959 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00791: Printing document sections

- page_id: `247047959`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047959/Printing+document+sections
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:49.082+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Sharing and exporting
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can print the loaded section of a Cameo Collaborator document as described below.

To print a section of a Cameo Collaborator document

1. Open a Cameo Collaborator document.
2. In the navigation pane, select the item whose information you want to print. The information is loaded in the content pane. Opening the navigation paneTo show the navigation pane, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the app bar.
3. Press Ctrl+P. The print window opens as displayed in the figure below.
4. Specify the printing settings, such as the number of copies, layout, and color.
5. Click the Print button.

The loaded section of a Cameo Collaborator document is now printed.

#### INFO: Printing in Model and Document views

Printing in Model and Document views

- If you print a document section in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Document view modes' space='MCS'] , all the content of the element selected in the navigation pane is printed.
- If you print a document section in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Document view modes' space='MCS'] , the loaded content starting with the content of the selected element is printed. The printed content may include information for several elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The print preview window of a document section.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The information is loaded in the content pane.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a0b32a9b-3347-4c15-a9c5-afb0b983ed8b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Opening the navigation pane</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To show the navigation pane, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show-hide_navigation_pane.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Printing document sections" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the app bar.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Press Ctrl+P. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247048042 space=MCS version=5 -->
## PAGE 00793: Product FAQ

- page_id: `247048042`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247048042/Product+FAQ
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-12-22T09:18:29.181+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > FAQ and Troubleshooting
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXPAND: How to address Apache Tomcat vulnerabilities found?

How to address Apache Tomcat vulnerabilities found?

Web Application Platform is a component of Teamwork Cloud. Apache Tomcat is the web server environment used by Web Application Platform. Frequent patching of Tomcat is needed to address the latest vulnerabilities (CVE) discovered.

For detailed instructions, refer to the[Upgrading and Patching Apache Tomcat](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCS/Hardening+Web+Application+Platform#HardeningWebApplicationPlatform-UpgradingandpatchingApacheTomcat)documentation:

- For Teamwork Cloud 22x and older, find the latest patch for Tomcat 9.X
- For Teamwork Cloud 24x and newer, find the latest patch for Tomcat 10.X.

Patching on Linux servers can be performed using a script that we provide. Both online and offline patching are supported. Windows Servers will require a manual patching process.

#### EXPAND: Why is Cassandra failing to start?

Why is Cassandra failing to start?

Cassandra startup issue could be due to several reasons. The service log should provide clues on what is causing the issue. Use the *journalctl -u cassandra* command to look through the service startup log for errors.

For JVM error, make sure the default Java version is set to support your Cassandra version (Java 11 for 4.X and Java 1.8 for 3.X).

For JNA errors, such as “failed to link C library against JNA” or fatal error SIGSEGV, system mount is the issue. Verify whether */tmp* and */dev/shm* are mounted with the *noexec* option (command: *findmnt /tmp*). Note that the Cassandra installation script will also check these mounts.

Cassandra uses the Java Native Access (JNA) library to access platform-specific shared libraries. During startup, both */tmp* and */dev/shm* are used for execution.

While */tmp* can be configured to another path, */dev/shm* must be remounted with *exec*. To set the *tmp* directory to an alternate path, find or create a path where Cassandra will have permission to execute in. Then, append the following line to the *cassandra-env.sh* file in */etc/cassandra/conf*.

```text

```

Before restarting Cassandra, make sure to delete all the existing commitlogs. Previous startup attempts would have created numerous commitlog files.

Note that if */tmp* or */dev/shm* is mounted as *noexec*, changing permission or ownership of the directory will not resolve the issue. The directory either has to be remounted with *noexec* removed or use the solution provided above.

Error “/etc/rc.d/init.d/functions: No such file or directory” is related to RHEL 9. However, in some configurations, it can occur on older releases. You can try to solve it by installing *initscripts*:

```bash

```

or by copying */etc/rc.d/init.d/functions*file from an older machine.

#### EXPAND: Where can I find log files?

Where can I find log files?

Use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Log file collection' space=''] to automatically gather and package all the relevant log files. Specific log file paths can be found in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='File locations' space=''].

#### EXPAND: How to install and use Temurin OpenJDK or another Java release on Linux?

How to install and use Temurin OpenJDK or another Java release on Linux?

Download the desired version of Temurin OpenJDK ([https://adoptium.net/temurin/releases/](https://adoptium.net/temurin/releases/)). For most cases, the JRE package is adequate.

Unpack the tar.gz file on the Linux server (*/opt*path is recommended)

Add the newly-installed Java release as an alternative:

```bash
/bin/java ]]>
```

Set as the default system Java:

```bash

```

#### EXPAND: How do I upgrade Teamwork Cloud to a newer version?

How do I upgrade Teamwork Cloud to a newer version?

Open the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Upgrade and data migration' space='']in a new tab and use it to determine what steps are required for the upgrade. Upgrading from older versions will require database migration and Cassandra upgrade. Minor upgrades will only involve uninstalling the current version and installing the latest version.

#### EXPAND: Does 2021x Refresh 2 work with Cassandra 4.1?

Does 2021x Refresh 2 work with Cassandra 4.1?

Teamwork Cloud 2021x Refresh 2 Hot Fix 5 supports Cassandra 4.0.X out of the box. For 2021x Refresh 2 to connect to Cassandra 4.1.X, add the following parameter to the end of the application.conf file in *<install_root>/<product>/configuration*.

```text

```

#### EXPAND: How to proceed with installation when encountering com.zerog.lax.LAX error?

How to proceed with installation when encountering com.zerog.lax.LAX error?

If the following error is encountered during installation:

```text

```

The best solution is to download the latest installer and installation scripts for your version. This issue is caused by newer versions of Java 11 and 17. Our scripts and installers (.bin) have been updated to address this issue.

If you are unable to use the latest installer and scripts, then add the following additional Java environment parameter when executing the installer. This environment parameter should either be added to the installation script, or on the command line if you are executing the .bin installer file directly.

```text

```

#### EXPAND: Is offline installation possible for Linux?

Is offline installation possible for Linux?

It is possible to install Teamwork Cloud without internet access, provided that the prerequisite packages are available locally. Access to a locally-managed repository server is a recommended for offline installation. Linux packages often have dependencies that are pulled and installed automatically when connecting to a repository. Manual download of Linux packages may require a trial-and-error process of retrieving dependencies.

###### Access to repository server

If you have access to a locally-managed repository server, go through the installation sections and download all prerequisite packages listed. If necessary, edit the installation scripts and replace all hyperlinks with local path to the downloaded package. Finally, execute the installation script. The local repository server will resolve all packages and dependencies needed during installation.

###### Manual installation

If your only option is to manually install all packages needed, then you will need to first download all the prerequisite packages from a machine with network access to either public or local package repositories. Then, as each package is installed, you will need to make a note of the dependencies required. This will be an iterative process in order to find all the dependencies that are needed to complete the installation.

To prepare for manual installation, a network-accessible machine is initially needed to download all the required packages. This machine should mirror the environment of the deployment server. A mirrored environment will accurately resolve package dependencies. Use the DNF Download Plugin (dnf-plugins-core) to retrieve packages.

```bash
]]>
```

The command structure above will download the main package and any dependencies that is required. Dependencies are dictated by the Linux environment. If the deployment server has a very different Linux environment than the download machine, then resolving package dependencies can be a tedious process.

#### EXPAND: What is causing the System Error when trying to access the web login page?

What is causing the System Error when trying to access the web login page?

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This error is typically due to an incorrect address entry in the authentication whitelist. Verify addresses specified for *authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist* in *authserver.properties* file are correct.

#### EXPAND: How to run Teamwork Cloud in FIPS Mode?

How to run Teamwork Cloud in FIPS Mode?

You may encounter an error message similar to the one shown below when running Teamwork Cloud in FIPS Mode. PKCS12 keystore creation and usage are blocked in FIPS Mode for Java 11 and older versions.

```text

```

First, make sure the initial installation was able to generate a self-signed certificate and keystore. Look for *keystore.p12* and *teamworkcloud.crt* files in *<install_root>/Certs*. If these files were not generated, run the *genkey* script in *<install_root>/TeamworkCloud/script/linux*. Check for the *keystore.p12* and *teamworkcloud.crt* files again and change ownership to *twcloud (*or other username that you chose during installation.) Once these two files are generated, version 2024x should be able to run in FIPS Mode.

If the genkey script is not able to generate the self-signed certificate and keystore, set the system Java version to 17 (*alternatives --config java*) and try again.

For 2022x Refresh 2, append the following line to the end of the *jvm.options* file located in *<install_root>/TeamworkCloud*.

```text

```

This configuration will allow Teamwork Cloud to use PKCS #12 keystore in FIPS Mode. Similar configurations will have to be set for Cassandra if encrypted communication is enabled.

Note that the Cassandra installation script will also check these mounts.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Cassandra uses the Java Native Access (JNA) library to access platform-specific shared libraries. During startup, both <em>/tmp</em> and <em>/dev/shm</em> are used for execution.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">While <em>/tmp</em> can be configured to another path, <em>/dev/shm</em> must be remounted with <em>exec</em>. Then, append the following line to the <em>cassandra-env.sh</em> file in <em>/etc/cassandra/conf</em>.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df342036-4948-4c5a-b491-701390e68173"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[JVM_OPTS="$JVM_OPTS -Djna.tmpdir=[NEW_PATH] -Djava.io.tmpdir=[NEW_PATH]"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Before restarting Cassandra, make sure to delete all the existing commitlogs.
